## Section 11: Search & Discovery — Full-Text + Geo + Filter Pipeline

### Phase 11.1: Database Search Infrastructure

**File: `supabase/migrations/005_search_infrastructure.sql`**

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- Search Infrastructure
-- Full-text search (tsvector) + geo-radius search + filter pipeline
-- 
-- ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS:
--
-- 1. FULL-TEXT over ILIKE:
--    ILIKE '%query%' = sequential scan O(N). Unacceptable at 10k+ rows.
--    tsvector GIN index = O(log N) + rank scoring. Required for production.
--
-- 2. GENERATED COLUMN for tsvector:
--    Computed at INSERT/UPDATE time, not at query time.
--    Eliminates per-query tsvector construction overhead.
--    Trade-off: Slightly larger row size (~50 bytes/row). Acceptable.
--
-- 3. COMBINED SEARCH FUNCTION:
--    Single RPC call handles: text query + geo filter + feature flags.
--    Avoids N+1 RPC round trips for filter combinations.
--    Returns distance_meters computed server-side (PostGIS sphere distance).
--
-- 4. SEARCH HISTORY:
--    Stored in client AsyncStorage only — NOT in the database.
--    Reason: Search history is session-local, PII-sensitive, and does not
--    need cross-device sync. Storing in DB requires GDPR compliance overhead.
-- ============================================================================

-- ============================================================================
-- ADD FULL-TEXT SEARCH VECTOR TO BATHROOMS
-- ============================================================================

-- Add tsvector generated column for full-text search
ALTER TABLE public.bathrooms
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS search_vector tsvector
    GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
      setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(place_name, '')), 'A') ||
      setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(address_line1, '')), 'B') ||
      setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(city, '')), 'B') ||
      setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(state, '')), 'C') ||
      setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(postal_code, '')), 'C')
    ) STORED;

-- GIN index for full-text search (required — without this, FTS is slower than ILIKE)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bathrooms_search_vector
  ON public.bathrooms USING GIN(search_vector);

-- ============================================================================
-- GENERAL PROXIMITY SEARCH FUNCTION
-- Used by the Search Tab for "find near me" + text queries
-- Distinct from search_bathrooms_accessible (which requires accessibility mode)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.search_bathrooms(
  -- Geographic parameters
  p_latitude        DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT NULL,
  p_longitude       DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT NULL,
  p_radius_meters   DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT 5000,

  -- Full-text search
  p_query           TEXT DEFAULT NULL,

  -- Feature filters
  p_is_accessible   BOOLEAN DEFAULT NULL,
  p_is_locked       BOOLEAN DEFAULT NULL,
  p_has_code        BOOLEAN DEFAULT NULL,
  p_is_customer_only BOOLEAN DEFAULT NULL,

  -- Pagination
  p_limit           INTEGER DEFAULT 25,
  p_offset          INTEGER DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id                UUID,
  place_name        TEXT,
  address_line1     TEXT,
  city              TEXT,
  state             TEXT,
  postal_code       TEXT,
  latitude          DOUBLE PRECISION,
  longitude         DOUBLE PRECISION,
  is_locked         BOOLEAN,
  is_accessible     BOOLEAN,
  is_customer_only  BOOLEAN,
  hours_json        JSONB,
  distance_meters   DOUBLE PRECISION,
  text_rank         REAL,
  has_code          BOOLEAN,
  code_confidence   NUMERIC,
  last_verified_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_point     GEOGRAPHY;
  v_tsquery   tsquery;
BEGIN
  -- Build geography point if coordinates provided
  IF p_latitude IS NOT NULL AND p_longitude IS NOT NULL THEN
    v_point := ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(p_longitude, p_latitude), 4326)::geography;
  END IF;

  -- Build tsquery from text input
  -- plainto_tsquery is injection-safe and handles multi-word queries
  -- e.g. "pike place market" → 'pike' & 'place' & 'market'
  IF p_query IS NOT NULL AND length(trim(p_query)) > 0 THEN
    v_tsquery := plainto_tsquery('english', trim(p_query));
  END IF;

  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    b.id,
    b.place_name,
    b.address_line1,
    b.city,
    b.state,
    b.postal_code,
    b.latitude,
    b.longitude,
    b.is_locked,
    b.is_accessible,
    b.is_customer_only,
    b.hours_json,

    -- Distance: NULL when no coordinates provided (text-only search)
    CASE
      WHEN v_point IS NOT NULL THEN ST_Distance(b.geom, v_point)
      ELSE NULL::DOUBLE PRECISION
    END AS distance_meters,

    -- Text relevance rank: 0.0 when no query (pure geo sort)
    CASE
      WHEN v_tsquery IS NOT NULL THEN ts_rank_cd(b.search_vector, v_tsquery)
      ELSE 0.0::REAL
    END AS text_rank,

    -- Code availability (from summary view)
    (cs.code_id IS NOT NULL)    AS has_code,
    cs.confidence_score         AS code_confidence,
    cs.last_verified_at,
    b.updated_at

  FROM public.bathrooms b
  LEFT JOIN public.v_bathroom_code_summary cs ON cs.bathroom_id = b.id

  WHERE
    -- Hard filter: active bathrooms only
    b.moderation_status = 'active'

    -- Geographic filter: only apply when coordinates provided
    AND (
      v_point IS NULL
      OR ST_DWithin(b.geom, v_point, p_radius_meters)
    )

    -- Full-text filter: only apply when query provided
    AND (
      v_tsquery IS NULL
      OR b.search_vector @@ v_tsquery
    )

    -- Feature flag filters (NULL = no filter applied)
    AND (p_is_accessible  IS NULL OR b.is_accessible  = p_is_accessible)
    AND (p_is_locked      IS NULL OR b.is_locked      = p_is_locked)
    AND (p_is_customer_only IS NULL OR b.is_customer_only = p_is_customer_only)

    -- Code availability filter
    AND (
      p_has_code IS NULL
      OR (p_has_code = TRUE  AND cs.code_id IS NOT NULL)
      OR (p_has_code = FALSE AND cs.code_id IS NULL)
    )

  ORDER BY
    -- Composite sort:
    -- 1. Text relevance (desc) when query present
    -- 2. Distance (asc) when coordinates present
    -- 3. Fallback: most recently updated
    CASE WHEN v_tsquery IS NOT NULL THEN ts_rank_cd(b.search_vector, v_tsquery) END DESC NULLS LAST,
    CASE WHEN v_point   IS NOT NULL THEN ST_Distance(b.geom, v_point) END ASC NULLS LAST,
    b.updated_at DESC

  LIMIT  LEAST(p_limit, 50)   -- Hard cap at 50 (prevent abuse)
  OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- SEARCH SUGGESTIONS FUNCTION
-- Returns typeahead suggestions as user types (debounced on client)
-- Returns place_name + city combinations for top 8 matches
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_search_suggestions(
  p_query         TEXT,
  p_latitude      DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT NULL,
  p_longitude     DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT NULL,
  p_limit         INTEGER DEFAULT 8
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  bathroom_id   UUID,
  place_name    TEXT,
  city          TEXT,
  state         TEXT,
  distance_meters DOUBLE PRECISION
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_point   GEOGRAPHY;
  v_tsquery tsquery;
BEGIN
  IF p_query IS NULL OR length(trim(p_query)) < 2 THEN
    RETURN; -- No suggestions for <2 chars (prevent noise)
  END IF;

  IF p_latitude IS NOT NULL AND p_longitude IS NOT NULL THEN
    v_point := ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(p_longitude, p_latitude), 4326)::geography;
  END IF;

  -- Use prefix matching for suggestions (user still typing)
  -- to_tsquery with :* suffix enables prefix matching on last token
  v_tsquery := to_tsquery(
    'english',
    concat(
      plainto_tsquery('english', trim(p_query))::text,
      ':*'  -- Prefix match on the last word
    )
  );

  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    b.id,
    b.place_name,
    b.city,
    b.state,
    CASE
      WHEN v_point IS NOT NULL THEN ST_Distance(b.geom, v_point)
      ELSE NULL::DOUBLE PRECISION
    END AS distance_meters
  FROM public.bathrooms b
  WHERE
    b.moderation_status = 'active'
    AND b.search_vector @@ v_tsquery
    AND (
      v_point IS NULL
      OR ST_DWithin(b.geom, v_point, 25000) -- 25km suggestion radius
    )
  ORDER BY
    ts_rank_cd(b.search_vector, v_tsquery) DESC,
    CASE WHEN v_point IS NOT NULL THEN ST_Distance(b.geom, v_point) END ASC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT LEAST(p_limit, 8);

EXCEPTION
  -- plainto_tsquery can throw on malformed input — return empty on error
  WHEN OTHERS THEN
    RETURN;
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- PERMISSIONS
-- ============================================================================

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.search_bathrooms TO anon, authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.get_search_suggestions TO anon, authenticated;
```

---

### Phase 11.2: TypeScript Search Domain Types

**File: `src/types/search.ts`**

```typescript
/**
 * Search Domain Types
 *
 * DESIGN CONSTRAINTS:
 * - SearchResult is a flattened projection of bathroom + code summary.
 *   It is NOT a BathroomListItem. They serve different surfaces.
 *   SearchResult is optimized for list rendering (pre-formatted address string,
 *   pre-computed distance label, relevance rank included).
 *
 * - SearchFilters are the canonical client-side filter state.
 *   They map 1:1 to the search_bathrooms() RPC parameters.
 *   Zod validator enforces this contract at the service boundary.
 *
 * - SearchHistoryEntry is persisted to AsyncStorage only (never DB).
 *   Max 20 entries, FIFO eviction, deduplication by normalized query string.
 */

// ============================================================================
// SEARCH RESULT
// ============================================================================

export interface SearchResult {
  id: string;
  place_name: string;
  address: string;           // Pre-formatted: "123 Main St, Seattle, WA"
  city: string | null;
  state: string | null;
  coordinates: {
    latitude: number;
    longitude: number;
  };
  distance_meters: number | null;
  distance_label: string | null;  // Pre-formatted: "0.3 mi" or "450 m"
  text_rank: number;
  flags: {
    is_locked: boolean | null;
    is_accessible: boolean | null;
    is_customer_only: boolean;
  };
  code_summary: {
    has_code: boolean;
    confidence: number | null;
    last_verified_at: string | null;
  };
  updated_at: string;
}

// ============================================================================
// SEARCH FILTERS
// ============================================================================

export interface SearchFilters {
  isAccessible: boolean | null;
  isLocked: boolean | null;
  hasCode: boolean | null;
  isCustomerOnly: boolean | null;
  radiusMeters: number;
}

export const DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS: SearchFilters = {
  isAccessible: null,
  isLocked: null,
  hasCode: null,
  isCustomerOnly: false,  // Default: exclude customer-only (most users want public)
  radiusMeters: 5000,
};

export const RADIUS_OPTIONS: Array<{ label: string; value: number }> = [
  { label: '0.5 mi', value: 800 },
  { label: '1 mi',   value: 1600 },
  { label: '3 mi',   value: 4800 },
  { label: '5 mi',   value: 8000 },
  { label: '10 mi',  value: 16000 },
];

// ============================================================================
// SEARCH HISTORY
// ============================================================================

export interface SearchHistoryEntry {
  id: string;                // nanoid for stable FlatList keys
  query: string;             // Raw user input
  normalizedQuery: string;   // Lowercased + trimmed (dedup key)
  timestamp: string;         // ISO 8601
  resultCount: number | null; // How many results this query returned
}

export const MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES = 20;

// ============================================================================
// SUGGESTIONS
// ============================================================================

export interface SearchSuggestion {
  bathroom_id: string;
  place_name: string;
  city: string | null;
  state: string | null;
  distance_meters: number | null;
  distance_label: string | null;
}

// ============================================================================
// SEARCH STATE MACHINE
// ============================================================================

export type SearchPhase =
  | 'idle'           // No query, showing history
  | 'typing'         // User actively typing, debounce in progress
  | 'suggesting'     // Showing typeahead suggestions
  | 'searching'      // Query committed, loading results
  | 'results'        // Showing results list
  | 'empty'          // Search returned 0 results
  | 'error';         // Network/DB error during search

// ============================================================================
// PAGINATED RESPONSE
// ============================================================================

export interface SearchPage {
  results: SearchResult[];
  offset: number;
  hasMore: boolean;   // results.length === PAGE_SIZE indicates potential next page
  query: string;
  filters: SearchFilters;
}

export const SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE = 25;
```

---

### Phase 11.3: Zod Validators

**File: `src/validators/search.ts`**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';
import { DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS, SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE } from '@/types/search';

/**
 * Search Validators
 *
 * INJECTION GUARD:
 * plainto_tsquery on the server handles SQL injection.
 * Client-side Zod adds a second layer: reject obviously malformed input
 * before it hits the network (latency + cost saving).
 *
 * MAX QUERY LENGTH:
 * 200 chars. plainto_tsquery degrades gracefully beyond this but there is
 * no legitimate search query longer than 200 chars. Truncating prevents
 * DoS via enormous tsquery construction on the DB server.
 */

export const searchQuerySchema = z
  .string()
  .min(1, 'Search query cannot be empty')
  .max(200, 'Search query too long')
  .transform((s) => s.trim());

export const searchFiltersSchema = z.object({
  isAccessible:     z.boolean().nullable().default(null),
  isLocked:         z.boolean().nullable().default(null),
  hasCode:          z.boolean().nullable().default(null),
  isCustomerOnly:   z.boolean().nullable().default(false),
  radiusMeters:     z.number().int().min(100).max(50000).default(5000),
});

export const searchParamsSchema = z.object({
  query:      searchQuerySchema.optional(),
  latitude:   z.number().min(-90).max(90).nullable().default(null),
  longitude:  z.number().min(-180).max(180).nullable().default(null),
  filters:    searchFiltersSchema.default(DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS),
  limit:      z.number().int().min(1).max(50).default(SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE),
  offset:     z.number().int().min(0).default(0),
});

export const suggestionQuerySchema = z
  .string()
  .min(2, 'Need at least 2 characters for suggestions')
  .max(100)
  .transform((s) => s.trim());

export type ValidatedSearchParams = z.infer<typeof searchParamsSchema>;
export type ValidatedSearchFilters = z.infer<typeof searchFiltersSchema>;

export function validateSearchParams(data: unknown): ValidatedSearchParams {
  return searchParamsSchema.parse(data);
}

export function validateSuggestionQuery(data: unknown): string {
  return suggestionQuerySchema.parse(data);
}
```

---

### Phase 11.4: Search Store (Zustand)

**File: `src/stores/searchStore.ts`**

```typescript
import { create } from 'zustand';
import { persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import type {
  SearchFilters,
  SearchHistoryEntry,
  SearchPhase,
} from '@/types/search';
import {
  DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS,
  MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES,
} from '@/types/search';

/**
 * SearchStore
 *
 * RESPONSIBILITIES:
 * 1. Track active query text and committed query (different — typing vs submitted)
 * 2. Persist search filter preferences across sessions
 * 3. Persist search history (AsyncStorage, max 20 entries, FIFO eviction)
 * 4. Drive search phase state machine (idle → typing → results)
 *
 * ACTIVE vs COMMITTED QUERY:
 * - activeQuery: Updates on every keystroke (drives suggestion debounce)
 * - committedQuery: Updates only on search submission (drives result fetch)
 * This prevents result queries from firing on every keystroke.
 *
 * HISTORY DEDUPLICATION:
 * Before pushing a new entry, normalize (lowercase + trim) and check if
 * normalizedQuery already exists. If yes, remove old entry and push new one
 * to front (most-recent semantics). This keeps history clean without exact
 * case matching ("Starbucks" and "starbucks" are the same entry).
 *
 * PERSISTENCE SCOPE:
 * - filters: persisted (user's filter preferences survive app restart)
 * - history: persisted (search history survives app restart)
 * - activeQuery, committedQuery, phase: NOT persisted (transient UI state)
 */

interface SearchStore {
  // Transient (not persisted)
  activeQuery: string;
  committedQuery: string;
  phase: SearchPhase;

  // Persisted
  filters: SearchFilters;
  history: SearchHistoryEntry[];

  // Actions
  setActiveQuery: (query: string) => void;
  commitQuery: (query: string) => void;
  clearQuery: () => void;
  setPhase: (phase: SearchPhase) => void;
  setFilter: <K extends keyof SearchFilters>(key: K, value: SearchFilters[K]) => void;
  resetFilters: () => void;
  pushHistory: (entry: Omit<SearchHistoryEntry, 'id' | 'timestamp'>) => void;
  removeHistory: (id: string) => void;
  clearHistory: () => void;
}

export const useSearchStore = create<SearchStore>()(
  persist(
    (set, get) => ({
      // Transient state (excluded from persistence via partialize below)
      activeQuery: '',
      committedQuery: '',
      phase: 'idle',

      // Persisted state
      filters: DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS,
      history: [],

      setActiveQuery: (query) =>
        set({
          activeQuery: query,
          phase: query.length === 0 ? 'idle' : 'typing',
        }),

      commitQuery: (query) => {
        const trimmed = query.trim();
        set({
          committedQuery: trimmed,
          activeQuery: trimmed,
          phase: trimmed.length > 0 ? 'searching' : 'idle',
        });
      },

      clearQuery: () =>
        set({
          activeQuery: '',
          committedQuery: '',
          phase: 'idle',
        }),

      setPhase: (phase) => set({ phase }),

      setFilter: (key, value) =>
        set((state) => ({
          filters: { ...state.filters, [key]: value },
        })),

      resetFilters: () => set({ filters: DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS }),

      /**
       * Push entry to history with FIFO eviction + deduplication
       *
       * ALGORITHM:
       * 1. Normalize incoming query
       * 2. Remove any existing entry with same normalizedQuery
       * 3. Prepend new entry (most recent first)
       * 4. Truncate to MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES
       *
       * EDGE CASE: Empty query → silently drop (no history entry for blank)
       */
      pushHistory: (entry) => {
        const { query } = entry;
        if (!query || query.trim().length === 0) return;

        const normalizedQuery = query.trim().toLowerCase();
        const id = `hist_${Date.now()}_${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 6)}`;

        set((state) => {
          // Deduplicate by normalizedQuery
          const deduped = state.history.filter(
            (h) => h.normalizedQuery !== normalizedQuery
          );

          const newEntry: SearchHistoryEntry = {
            id,
            query: query.trim(),
            normalizedQuery,
            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
            resultCount: entry.resultCount,
          };

          // Prepend + truncate
          const updated = [newEntry, ...deduped].slice(0, MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES);

          return { history: updated };
        });
      },

      removeHistory: (id) =>
        set((state) => ({
          history: state.history.filter((h) => h.id !== id),
        })),

      clearHistory: () => set({ history: [] }),
    }),
    {
      name: 'peedom-search-store',
      storage: createJSONStorage(() => AsyncStorage),
      // Only persist filters and history — transient state is intentionally excluded
      partialize: (state) => ({
        filters: state.filters,
        history: state.history,
      }),
    }
  )
);

// Stable selectors
export const selectActiveFiltersCount = (state: SearchStore): number =>
  Object.entries(state.filters).filter(([key, value]) => {
    if (key === 'radiusMeters') return value !== DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS.radiusMeters;
    if (key === 'isCustomerOnly') return value !== DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS.isCustomerOnly;
    return value !== null;
  }).length;

export const selectHasActiveFilters = (state: SearchStore): boolean =>
  selectActiveFiltersCount(state) > 0;
```

---

### Phase 11.5: Search Service

**File: `src/services/SearchService.ts`**

```typescript
import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
import { validateSearchParams, validateSuggestionQuery } from '@/validators/search';
import type {
  SearchResult,
  SearchSuggestion,
  SearchFilters,
  SearchPage,
} from '@/types/search';
import { SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE } from '@/types/search';

/**
 * SearchService
 *
 * DESIGN CONTRACTS:
 * - All public methods validate input before touching the network
 * - All DB rows are transformed into domain types before returning
 * - No raw Supabase row shapes leak past this service boundary
 * - Pagination is offset-based (cursor-based would require stable sort key
 *   across pages — problematic with combined text_rank + distance sort)
 *
 * DISTANCE FORMATTING:
 * Computed server-side (meters), formatted client-side (mi/km).
 * Locale detection: Always miles for US (country_code = 'US').
 * Could be extended with i18n but YAGNI for MVP.
 *
 * EMPTY QUERY + NO LOCATION:
 * This is a valid state (user opened search tab, no input, no GPS).
 * Returns empty results rather than throwing. UI handles via 'idle' phase.
 *
 * NULL vs UNDEFINED:
 * Supabase RPC receives null for absent parameters.
 * Zod defaults handle undefined → null transformation at the service boundary.
 */

class SearchService {
  /**
   * Execute full search with optional text query + geo + filters
   *
   * PAGINATION CONTRACT:
   * - First page: offset=0
   * - Subsequent pages: offset += PAGE_SIZE
   * - hasMore: inferred from result count (== PAGE_SIZE → likely more)
   * - Final page: results.length < PAGE_SIZE
   *
   * CONCURRENCY:
   * TanStack Query deduplicates identical concurrent requests.
   * No additional deduplication needed here.
   *
   * COST CONTROL:
   * Hard cap at 50 results per page enforced in SQL (LEAST(p_limit, 50)).
   * Client cannot bypass via large p_limit value.
   */
  async search(params: {
    query?: string;
    latitude?: number | null;
    longitude?: number | null;
    filters: SearchFilters;
    offset?: number;
  }): Promise<SearchPage> {
    const validated = validateSearchParams({
      query:      params.query,
      latitude:   params.latitude ?? null,
      longitude:  params.longitude ?? null,
      filters:    params.filters,
      limit:      SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE,
      offset:     params.offset ?? 0,
    });

    // Both query and location are absent → return empty (prevent full-table scan)
    if (!validated.query && !validated.latitude && !validated.longitude) {
      return {
        results: [],
        offset: 0,
        hasMore: false,
        query: '',
        filters: params.filters,
      };
    }

    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('search_bathrooms', {
      p_latitude:         validated.latitude,
      p_longitude:        validated.longitude,
      p_radius_meters:    validated.filters.radiusMeters,
      p_query:            validated.query ?? null,
      p_is_accessible:    validated.filters.isAccessible,
      p_is_locked:        validated.filters.isLocked,
      p_has_code:         validated.filters.hasCode,
      p_is_customer_only: validated.filters.isCustomerOnly,
      p_limit:            validated.limit,
      p_offset:           validated.offset,
    });

    if (error) {
      throw new Error(`SEARCH_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    const rows = (data || []) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
    const results = rows.map((row) => this.transformRow(row));

    return {
      results,
      offset:   validated.offset,
      hasMore:  results.length === SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE,
      query:    validated.query ?? '',
      filters:  params.filters,
    };
  }

  /**
   * Fetch typeahead suggestions (debounced on caller)
   *
   * PERFORMANCE:
   * Suggestions use prefix matching (to_tsquery :* suffix).
   * Limited to 8 results. Expected p99 latency: <50ms with GIN index.
   *
   * FAILURE MODE:
   * Any error returns empty array (suggestions are non-critical).
   * Thrown errors from Supabase are caught and logged, never surfaced to user.
   */
  async getSuggestions(params: {
    query: string;
    latitude?: number | null;
    longitude?: number | null;
  }): Promise<SearchSuggestion[]> {
    let validatedQuery: string;

    try {
      validatedQuery = validateSuggestionQuery(params.query);
    } catch {
      return []; // <2 chars or invalid — silent fail
    }

    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('get_search_suggestions', {
      p_query:     validatedQuery,
      p_latitude:  params.latitude ?? null,
      p_longitude: params.longitude ?? null,
      p_limit:     8,
    });

    if (error) {
      console.warn('SearchService.getSuggestions failed:', error.message);
      return [];
    }

    const rows = (data || []) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
    return rows.map((row) => ({
      bathroom_id:     row.bathroom_id as string,
      place_name:      row.place_name as string,
      city:            (row.city as string) ?? null,
      state:           (row.state as string) ?? null,
      distance_meters: (row.distance_meters as number) ?? null,
      distance_label:  row.distance_meters != null
        ? this.formatDistance(row.distance_meters as number)
        : null,
    }));
  }

  /**
   * Transform raw DB row into SearchResult domain type
   *
   * ADDRESS FORMATTING:
   * Builds "123 Main St, Seattle, WA 98101" from nullable components.
   * Gracefully handles partial data (city-only, state-only, etc.).
   */
  private transformRow(row: Record<string, unknown>): SearchResult {
    const addressParts: string[] = [];
    if (row.address_line1) addressParts.push(row.address_line1 as string);

    const cityState: string[] = [];
    if (row.city) cityState.push(row.city as string);
    if (row.state) cityState.push(row.state as string);
    if (cityState.length) addressParts.push(cityState.join(', '));
    if (row.postal_code && !row.city) addressParts.push(row.postal_code as string);

    return {
      id:          row.id as string,
      place_name:  row.place_name as string,
      address:     addressParts.join(', ') || 'Address unavailable',
      city:        (row.city as string) ?? null,
      state:       (row.state as string) ?? null,
      coordinates: {
        latitude:  row.latitude as number,
        longitude: row.longitude as number,
      },
      distance_meters: (row.distance_meters as number) ?? null,
      distance_label:  row.distance_meters != null
        ? this.formatDistance(row.distance_meters as number)
        : null,
      text_rank:   (row.text_rank as number) ?? 0,
      flags: {
        is_locked:        (row.is_locked as boolean) ?? null,
        is_accessible:    (row.is_accessible as boolean) ?? null,
        is_customer_only: (row.is_customer_only as boolean) ?? false,
      },
      code_summary: {
        has_code:        (row.has_code as boolean) ?? false,
        confidence:      (row.code_confidence as number) ?? null,
        last_verified_at: (row.last_verified_at as string) ?? null,
      },
      updated_at: row.updated_at as string,
    };
  }

  /**
   * Format distance in meters to human-readable string
   * Uses imperial (miles) for US context; extend for i18n as needed
   */
  private formatDistance(meters: number): string {
    const miles = meters / 1609.34;
    if (miles < 0.1) return `${Math.round(meters)} m`;
    if (miles < 10) return `${miles.toFixed(1)} mi`;
    return `${Math.round(miles)} mi`;
  }
}

export const searchService = new SearchService();
```

### Phase 11.6: TanStack Query Hooks

**File: `src/hooks/useSearch.ts`**

```typescript
import {
  useInfiniteQuery,
  useQuery,
  useQueryClient,
  keepPreviousData,
} from '@tanstack/react-query';
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { searchService } from '@/services/SearchService';
import { useSearchStore } from '@/stores/searchStore';
import type { SearchFilters, SearchPage } from '@/types/search';
import { SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE } from '@/types/search';

/**
 * Search Query Key Factory
 *
 * Hierarchical structure enables surgical invalidation:
 * - Invalidate ['search'] → clears ALL search cache (filter reset)
 * - Invalidate ['search', 'results', query] → clears one query's pages
 * - Invalidate ['search', 'suggestions'] → clears all suggestion caches
 */
const searchKeys = {
  all:         ['search'] as const,
  results:     (query: string, lat: number | null, lng: number | null, filters: SearchFilters) =>
    [...searchKeys.all, 'results', query, lat, lng, filters] as const,
  suggestions: (query: string, lat: number | null, lng: number | null) =>
    [...searchKeys.all, 'suggestions', query, lat, lng] as const,
};

/**
 * useSearchResults
 *
 * Infinite query for paginated search results.
 *
 * INFINITE QUERY DESIGN:
 * - getNextPageParam: Returns next offset if hasMore, else undefined
 * - initialPageParam: 0
 * - select: Flattens pages into single results array for UI consumption
 *
 * STALE TIME RATIONALE:
 * 2 minutes. Bathrooms don't change frequently enough to justify shorter.
 * Realtime hook (Section 10) handles live updates for map; search results
 * are intentionally slightly stale (user expectation: search = snapshot).
 *
 * KEEP PREVIOUS DATA:
 * When query changes, old results remain visible during loading.
 * Prevents jarring empty flash between searches.
 * TanStack v5: Use placeholderData: keepPreviousData
 *
 * ENABLED GUARD:
 * Query fires only when committedQuery OR location is present.
 * Prevents full-table scan RPC call on empty search tab mount.
 *
 * @param latitude  - User location (nullable if permission denied)
 * @param longitude - User location (nullable if permission denied)
 */
export function useSearchResults(
  latitude: number | null,
  longitude: number | null
) {
  const committedQuery = useSearchStore((s) => s.committedQuery);
  const filters        = useSearchStore((s) => s.filters);
  const setPhase       = useSearchStore((s) => s.setPhase);
  const pushHistory    = useSearchStore((s) => s.pushHistory);

  const hasInput = committedQuery.length > 0 || (latitude !== null && longitude !== null);

  return useInfiniteQuery({
    queryKey: searchKeys.results(committedQuery, latitude, longitude, filters),

    queryFn: async ({ pageParam }) => {
      const page = await searchService.search({
        query:     committedQuery || undefined,
        latitude,
        longitude,
        filters,
        offset:    pageParam,
      });

      // Side effect: push to history on first page of text queries
      if (pageParam === 0 && committedQuery.length > 0) {
        pushHistory({
          query:       committedQuery,
          normalizedQuery: committedQuery.toLowerCase(),
          resultCount: page.results.length,
        });

        // Update phase based on result count
        setPhase(page.results.length === 0 ? 'empty' : 'results');
      }

      return page;
    },

    initialPageParam: 0,
    getNextPageParam: (lastPage: SearchPage) =>
      lastPage.hasMore ? lastPage.offset + SEARCH_PAGE_SIZE : undefined,

    enabled: hasInput,
    staleTime: 2 * 60 * 1000,
    placeholderData: keepPreviousData,

    // On error: transition phase to error state
    // Note: React Query error handling is in onError callback (removed in v5)
    // Error state is read from the hook's `isError` flag in the component
  });
}

/**
 * useSearchSuggestions
 *
 * Typeahead suggestions — debounced via staleTime + enabled guard.
 *
 * DEBOUNCE STRATEGY:
 * We do NOT use lodash debounce here. Instead:
 * - enabled: only fires when activeQuery.length >= 2
 * - staleTime: 30s (suggestions cached — same prefix reuses cache instantly)
 * - The component handles keystroke debounce via a 300ms timeout before
 *   calling setActiveQuery. This keeps debounce logic in the component
 *   where it's visible, not hidden in the hook.
 *
 * SUGGESTION vs RESULT QUERIES:
 * Suggestions use a separate query key → do not interfere with result cache.
 * Clearing suggestions (pressing X) invalidates only suggestion keys.
 *
 * STALE TIME: 30s. Prefix suggestions for "Starbuck" won't change in 30s.
 */
export function useSearchSuggestions(
  latitude: number | null,
  longitude: number | null
) {
  const activeQuery = useSearchStore((s) => s.activeQuery);

  return useQuery({
    queryKey:  searchKeys.suggestions(activeQuery, latitude, longitude),
    queryFn:   () =>
      searchService.getSuggestions({
        query:     activeQuery,
        latitude,
        longitude,
      }),
    enabled:   activeQuery.length >= 2,
    staleTime: 30 * 1000,
    // Suggestions never cause phase transitions — purely cosmetic
  });
}

/**
 * useSearchActions
 *
 * Stable action callbacks for search interactions.
 * Centralizes side effects: store updates + query invalidation.
 *
 * DESIGN: Extracted from components to prevent inline arrow function
 * re-creation on every render, which would break memo() on child components.
 */
export function useSearchActions(
  latitude: number | null,
  longitude: number | null
) {
  const queryClient    = useQueryClient();
  const commitQuery    = useSearchStore((s) => s.commitQuery);
  const clearQuery     = useSearchStore((s) => s.clearQuery);
  const setFilter      = useSearchStore((s) => s.setFilter);
  const resetFilters   = useSearchStore((s) => s.resetFilters);
  const filters        = useSearchStore((s) => s.filters);

  /**
   * Submit a search query
   * Commits the query and invalidates result cache for fresh fetch
   */
  const submitSearch = useCallback(
    (query: string) => {
      commitQuery(query);
      // Invalidate existing results for this query to force fresh fetch
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({
        queryKey: searchKeys.results(query, latitude, longitude, filters),
        refetchType: 'active',
      });
    },
    [commitQuery, queryClient, latitude, longitude, filters]
  );

  /**
   * Clear search and return to idle state
   */
  const clearSearch = useCallback(() => {
    clearQuery();
    // Invalidate all suggestion caches
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: searchKeys.all });
  }, [clearQuery, queryClient]);

  /**
   * Update a filter and immediately re-run current search
   */
  const updateFilter = useCallback(
    <K extends keyof SearchFilters>(key: K, value: SearchFilters[K]) => {
      setFilter(key, value);
      // Filter change → existing result cache is stale
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({
        queryKey: [...searchKeys.all, 'results'],
        refetchType: 'active',
      });
    },
    [setFilter, queryClient]
  );

  /**
   * Reset all filters and re-run search
   */
  const clearFilters = useCallback(() => {
    resetFilters();
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: [...searchKeys.all, 'results'],
      refetchType: 'active',
    });
  }, [resetFilters, queryClient]);

  return { submitSearch, clearSearch, updateFilter, clearFilters };
}
```

### Phase 11.7: UI Components

**File: `src/components/search/SearchBar.tsx`**

```typescript
import { memo, useRef, useCallback, useState } from 'react';
import {
  View,
  TextInput,
  Text,
  Pressable,
  StyleSheet,
  Platform,
} from 'react-native';
import { useSearchStore } from '@/stores/searchStore';

/**
 * SearchBar
 *
 * DEBOUNCE IMPLEMENTATION:
 * 300ms timeout on keystroke before calling setActiveQuery.
 * Using useRef<NodeJS.Timeout> for the timeout ID — not useState —
 * because clearing a timeout should not trigger a re-render.
 *
 * RE-RENDER SURFACE:
 * Only re-renders when: localValue changes (controlled input).
 * Parent store updates do NOT cause re-render here (no store selectors in this component).
 * This is intentional: SearchBar is a pure input widget.
 *
 * CLEAR BUTTON:
 * Appears when localValue.length > 0.
 * Clears both local state AND committed query in store.
 *
 * ACCESSIBILY:
 * - accessibilityRole="search" on the outer View
 * - returnKeyType="search" on TextInput
 * - Clear button has explicit accessibilityLabel
 */

interface SearchBarProps {
  onSubmit:        (query: string) => void;
  onQueryChange:   (query: string) => void;  // For suggestion debounce
  onClear:         () => void;
  placeholder?:    string;
  autoFocus?:      boolean;
}

export const SearchBar = memo(function SearchBar({
  onSubmit,
  onQueryChange,
  onClear,
  placeholder = 'Search bathrooms...',
  autoFocus   = false,
}: SearchBarProps) {
  const [localValue, setLocalValue] = useState('');
  const debounceRef = useRef<NodeJS.Timeout | null>(null);
  const inputRef    = useRef<TextInput>(null);

  const handleChangeText = useCallback(
    (text: string) => {
      setLocalValue(text);

      // Clear existing debounce timeout
      if (debounceRef.current) {
        clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
      }

      if (text.length === 0) {
        // Immediate clear — no debounce needed
        onQueryChange('');
        return;
      }

      // Debounce suggestion fetch: 300ms
      debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {
        onQueryChange(text);
      }, 300);
    },
    [onQueryChange]
  );

  const handleSubmitEditing = useCallback(() => {
    if (debounceRef.current) {
      clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
    }
    const trimmed = localValue.trim();
    if (trimmed.length > 0) {
      onSubmit(trimmed);
    }
  }, [localValue, onSubmit]);

  const handleClear = useCallback(() => {
    if (debounceRef.current) {
      clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
    }
    setLocalValue('');
    onClear();
    inputRef.current?.focus();
  }, [onClear]);

  return (
    <View
      style={styles.container}
      accessibilityRole="search"
    >
      {/* Search Icon */}
      <Text style={styles.searchIcon} accessibilityElementsHidden>
        🔍
      </Text>

      <TextInput
        ref={inputRef}
        style={styles.input}
        value={localValue}
        onChangeText={handleChangeText}
        onSubmitEditing={handleSubmitEditing}
        placeholder={placeholder}
        placeholderTextColor="#9ca3af"
        returnKeyType="search"
        autoCapitalize="none"
        autoCorrect={false}
        autoFocus={autoFocus}
        clearButtonMode="never"  // We use custom clear button
        accessibilityLabel="Bathroom search input"
        accessibilityHint="Type to search by name or address, then press Search"
      />

      {/* Clear Button */}
      {localValue.length > 0 && (
        <Pressable
          style={styles.clearButton}
          onPress={handleClear}
          hitSlop={{ top: 8, bottom: 8, left: 8, right: 8 }}
          accessibilityLabel="Clear search"
          accessibilityRole="button"
        >
          <Text style={styles.clearIcon}>✕</Text>
        </Pressable>
      )}
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flexDirection:   'row',
    alignItems:      'center',
    backgroundColor: '#f3f4f6',
    borderRadius:    12,
    paddingHorizontal: 12,
    paddingVertical:   Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 10 : 6,
    gap: 8,
  },
  searchIcon: {
    fontSize: 16,
  },
  input: {
    flex:     1,
    fontSize: 16,
    color:    '#111827',
    padding:  0,  // Reset Android default padding
  },
  clearButton: {
    padding: 2,
  },
  clearIcon: {
    fontSize:   14,
    color:      '#6b7280',
    fontWeight: '600',
  },
});
```

**File: `src/components/search/SearchResultCard.tsx`**

```typescript
import { memo, useCallback } from 'react';
import { View, Text, Pressable, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import type { SearchResult } from '@/types/search';

/**
 * SearchResultCard
 *
 * MEMOIZATION:
 * Memoized with custom comparison — re-renders only when result.id or
 * code_summary.confidence changes (the only values that update post-fetch).
 *
 * LAYOUT:
 * ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
 * │ Place Name                    0.3 mi     │
 * │ 123 Main St, Seattle, WA                 │
 * │ [♿ Accessible] [🔐 Has Code]             │
 * └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
 *
 * ACCESSIBILITY:
 * - Entire card is one focusable element (not nested touchables)
 * - accessibilityLabel includes all visible info for VoiceOver
 * - Badge icons are accessibilityElementsHidden (label covers them)
 */

interface SearchResultCardProps {
  result:  SearchResult;
  onPress: (id: string) => void;
}

export const SearchResultCard = memo(
  function SearchResultCard({ result, onPress }: SearchResultCardProps) {
    const handlePress = useCallback(() => onPress(result.id), [result.id, onPress]);

    // Construct semantic a11y label covering all visible info
    const a11yLabel = buildA11yLabel(result);

    return (
      <Pressable
        style={({ pressed }) => [styles.card, pressed && styles.cardPressed]}
        onPress={handlePress}
        accessibilityLabel={a11yLabel}
        accessibilityRole="button"
        accessibilityHint="Tap to view bathroom details"
      >
        {/* Header Row */}
        <View style={styles.header}>
          <Text style={styles.name} numberOfLines={1}>
            {result.place_name}
          </Text>
          {result.distance_label && (
            <Text
              style={styles.distance}
              accessibilityElementsHidden
            >
              {result.distance_label}
            </Text>
          )}
        </View>

        {/* Address */}
        <Text style={styles.address} numberOfLines={1}>
          {result.address}
        </Text>

        {/* Feature Badges */}
        {(result.flags.is_accessible ||
          result.code_summary.has_code ||
          result.flags.is_locked === false) && (
          <View style={styles.badges} accessibilityElementsHidden>
            {result.flags.is_accessible && (
              <View style={[styles.badge, styles.badgeAccessible]}>
                <Text style={styles.badgeText}>♿ Accessible</Text>
              </View>
            )}
            {result.code_summary.has_code && (
              <View style={[styles.badge, styles.badgeCode]}>
                <Text style={styles.badgeText}>
                  🔑 Code{' '}
                  {result.code_summary.confidence !== null
                    ? `${Math.round(result.code_summary.confidence)}%`
                    : ''}
                </Text>
              </View>
            )}
            {result.flags.is_locked === false && (
              <View style={[styles.badge, styles.badgeUnlocked]}>
                <Text style={styles.badgeText}>🔓 Unlocked</Text>
              </View>
            )}
          </View>
        )}
      </Pressable>
    );
  },
  // Custom memo comparison: only re-render on data changes
  (prev, next) =>
    prev.result.id === next.result.id &&
    prev.result.code_summary.confidence === next.result.code_summary.confidence &&
    prev.onPress === next.onPress
);

function buildA11yLabel(result: SearchResult): string {
  const parts = [result.place_name];
  if (result.distance_label) parts.push(`${result.distance_label} away`);
  parts.push(result.address);
  if (result.flags.is_accessible)    parts.push('wheelchair accessible');
  if (result.code_summary.has_code)  parts.push('access code available');
  if (result.flags.is_locked === false) parts.push('unlocked');
  return parts.join(', ');
}

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  card: {
    backgroundColor:  '#ffffff',
    borderRadius:     10,
    padding:          14,
    marginHorizontal: 16,
    marginVertical:   5,
    borderWidth:      1,
    borderColor:      '#e5e7eb',
    shadowColor:      '#000',
    shadowOffset:     { width: 0, height: 1 },
    shadowOpacity:    0.04,
    shadowRadius:     2,
    elevation:        1,
  },
  cardPressed: {
    opacity:          0.75,
    backgroundColor:  '#f9fafb',
  },
  header: {
    flexDirection:    'row',
    alignItems:       'center',
    marginBottom:     4,
    gap:              8,
  },
  name: {
    flex:       1,
    fontSize:   16,
    fontWeight: '600',
    color:      '#111827',
  },
  distance: {
    fontSize:   13,
    color:      '#6b7280',
    fontWeight: '500',
  },
  address: {
    fontSize:     14,
    color:        '#6b7280',
    marginBottom: 8,
  },
  badges: {
    flexDirection: 'row',
    gap:           6,
    flexWrap:      'wrap',
  },
  badge: {
    paddingHorizontal: 8,
    paddingVertical:   3,
    borderRadius:      12,
  },
  badgeAccessible: { backgroundColor: '#d1fae5' },
  badgeCode:       { backgroundColor: '#dbeafe' },
  badgeUnlocked:   { backgroundColor: '#fef3c7' },
  badgeText: {
    fontSize:   12,
    fontWeight: '500',
    color:      '#374151',
  },
});
```

**File: `src/components/search/SearchHistoryList.tsx`**

```typescript
import { memo, useCallback } from 'react';
import { View, Text, FlatList, Pressable, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import { useSearchStore } from '@/stores/searchStore';
import type { SearchHistoryEntry } from '@/types/search';

/**
 * SearchHistoryList
 *
 * Renders persisted search history when search phase is 'idle'.
 * FlatList with memoized items for performance (history can have up to 20 entries).
 *
 * SWIPE TO DELETE:
 * Not implemented in MVP (requires react-native-swipe-list-view or gesture handler
 * implementation). Individual delete via trash icon on each row instead.
 */

interface SearchHistoryListProps {
  onSelect: (query: string) => void;
}

export const SearchHistoryList = memo(function SearchHistoryList({
  onSelect,
}: SearchHistoryListProps) {
  const history      = useSearchStore((s) => s.history);
  const removeEntry  = useSearchStore((s) => s.removeHistory);
  const clearAll     = useSearchStore((s) => s.clearHistory);

  const renderItem = useCallback(
    ({ item }: { item: SearchHistoryEntry }) => (
      <HistoryRow
        entry={item}
        onSelect={onSelect}
        onRemove={removeEntry}
      />
    ),
    [onSelect, removeEntry]
  );

  const keyExtractor = useCallback(
    (item: SearchHistoryEntry) => item.id,
    []
  );

  if (history.length === 0) return null;

  return (
    <View style={styles.container}>
      <View style={styles.header}>
        <Text style={styles.title}>Recent Searches</Text>
        <Pressable
          onPress={clearAll}
          hitSlop={{ top: 8, bottom: 8, left: 8, right: 8 }}
          accessibilityLabel="Clear all search history"
          accessibilityRole="button"
        >
          <Text style={styles.clearAll}>Clear all</Text>
        </Pressable>
      </View>

      <FlatList
        data={history}
        renderItem={renderItem}
        keyExtractor={keyExtractor}
        scrollEnabled={false}   // Parent ScrollView handles scroll
        removeClippedSubviews   // Memory optimization for long history
      />
    </View>
  );
});

interface HistoryRowProps {
  entry:    SearchHistoryEntry;
  onSelect: (query: string) => void;
  onRemove: (id: string) => void;
}

const HistoryRow = memo(function HistoryRow({
  entry,
  onSelect,
  onRemove,
}: HistoryRowProps) {
  const handleSelect = useCallback(() => onSelect(entry.query), [entry.query, onSelect]);
  const handleRemove = useCallback(() => onRemove(entry.id),    [entry.id,    onRemove]);

  return (
    <View style={styles.row}>
      <Pressable
        style={styles.rowContent}
        onPress={handleSelect}
        accessibilityLabel={`Search again: ${entry.query}`}
        accessibilityRole="button"
      >
        <Text style={styles.clockIcon} accessibilityElementsHidden>🕐</Text>
        <View style={styles.rowText}>
          <Text style={styles.query}>{entry.query}</Text>
          {entry.resultCount !== null && (
            <Text style={styles.resultCount}>
              {entry.resultCount} result{entry.resultCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
            </Text>
          )}
        </View>
      </Pressable>

      <Pressable
        style={styles.removeButton}
        onPress={handleRemove}
        hitSlop={{ top: 8, bottom: 8, left: 8, right: 8 }}
        accessibilityLabel={`Remove ${entry.query} from history`}
        accessibilityRole="button"
      >
        <Text style={styles.removeIcon}>✕</Text>
      </Pressable>
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    paddingTop: 16,
  },
  header: {
    flexDirection:    'row',
    justifyContent:   'space-between',
    alignItems:       'center',
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    marginBottom:     8,
  },
  title: {
    fontSize:   14,
    fontWeight: '600',
    color:      '#374151',
    textTransform: 'uppercase',
    letterSpacing: 0.5,
  },
  clearAll: {
    fontSize: 14,
    color:    '#2563eb',
  },
  row: {
    flexDirection: 'row',
    alignItems:    'center',
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical:   11,
    borderBottomWidth: 1,
    borderBottomColor: '#f3f4f6',
  },
  rowContent: {
    flexDirection: 'row',
    alignItems:    'center',
    flex:          1,
    gap:           10,
  },
  clockIcon: {
    fontSize: 16,
  },
  rowText: {
    flex: 1,
  },
  query: {
    fontSize: 15,
    color:    '#111827',
  },
  resultCount: {
    fontSize:   12,
    color:      '#9ca3af',
    marginTop:  2,
  },
  removeButton: {
    paddingLeft: 12,
  },
  removeIcon: {
    fontSize: 13,
    color:    '#9ca3af',
  },
});
```

**File: `src/components/search/SearchFilterChips.tsx`**

```typescript
import { memo, useCallback } from 'react';
import { View, Text, ScrollView, Pressable, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import { useSearchStore, selectActiveFiltersCount } from '@/stores/searchStore';
import type { SearchFilters } from '@/types/search';
import { RADIUS_OPTIONS } from '@/types/search';

/**
 * SearchFilterChips
 *
 * Horizontal scrollable chip bar for active filter display and quick toggle.
 * Renders below the SearchBar, above results.
 *
 * SCROLL DIRECTION: Horizontal (chips overflow on small screens).
 * showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false} for clean UI.
 *
 * ACTIVE FILTER BADGE:
 * A "Filters (N)" chip at the start shows active filter count.
 * Tapping it opens a more detailed filter sheet (future enhancement).
 * For now: tapping it resets all filters.
 */

interface SearchFilterChipsProps {
  onUpdateFilter: <K extends keyof SearchFilters>(key: K, value: SearchFilters[K]) => void;
  onClearFilters: () => void;
}

export const SearchFilterChips = memo(function SearchFilterChips({
  onUpdateFilter,
  onClearFilters,
}: SearchFilterChipsProps) {
  const filters      = useSearchStore((s) => s.filters);
  const activeCount  = useSearchStore(selectActiveFiltersCount);

  return (
    <ScrollView
      horizontal
      showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}
      contentContainerStyle={styles.scrollContent}
      style={styles.scroll}
      accessibilityLabel="Search filter chips"
    >
      {/* Active filter reset chip */}
      {activeCount > 0 && (
        <FilterChip
          label={`Filters (${activeCount})`}
          active
          onPress={onClearFilters}
          accessibilityLabel={`${activeCount} filters active, tap to reset`}
        />
      )}

      {/* Accessible Only */}
      <FilterChip
        label="♿ Accessible"
        active={filters.isAccessible === true}
        onPress={() =>
          onUpdateFilter(
            'isAccessible',
            filters.isAccessible === true ? null : true
          )
        }
        accessibilityLabel={`Accessible only filter, ${
          filters.isAccessible === true ? 'active' : 'inactive'
        }`}
      />

      {/* Has Code */}
      <FilterChip
        label="🔑 Has Code"
        active={filters.hasCode === true}
        onPress={() =>
          onUpdateFilter('hasCode', filters.hasCode === true ? null : true)
        }
        accessibilityLabel={`Has access code filter, ${
          filters.hasCode === true ? 'active' : 'inactive'
        }`}
      />

      {/* Unlocked */}
      <FilterChip
        label="🔓 Unlocked"
        active={filters.isLocked === false}
        onPress={() =>
          onUpdateFilter('isLocked', filters.isLocked === false ? null : false)
        }
        accessibilityLabel={`Unlocked bathrooms filter, ${
          filters.isLocked === false ? 'active' : 'inactive'
        }`}
      />

      {/* Radius chips */}
      {RADIUS_OPTIONS.map((option) => (
        <FilterChip
          key={option.value}
          label={option.label}
          active={filters.radiusMeters === option.value}
          onPress={() => onUpdateFilter('radiusMeters', option.value)}
          accessibilityLabel={`Search within ${option.label} radius, ${
            filters.radiusMeters === option.value ? 'selected' : 'inactive'
          }`}
        />
      ))}
    </ScrollView>
  );
});

interface FilterChipProps {
  label:              string;
  active:             boolean;
  onPress:            () => void;
  accessibilityLabel: string;
}

const FilterChip = memo(function FilterChip({
  label,
  active,
  onPress,
  accessibilityLabel,
}: FilterChipProps) {
  return (
    <Pressable
      style={({ pressed }) => [
        styles.chip,
        active  && styles.chipActive,
        pressed && styles.chipPressed,
      ]}
      onPress={onPress}
      accessibilityLabel={accessibilityLabel}
      accessibilityRole="button"
      accessibilityState={{ selected: active }}
    >
      <Text style={[styles.chipText, active && styles.chipTextActive]}>
        {label}
      </Text>
    </Pressable>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  scroll: {
    flexGrow: 0,
  },
  scrollContent: {
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical:   8,
    gap:               8,
  },
  chip: {
    paddingHorizontal: 12,
    paddingVertical:   7,
    borderRadius:      20,
    borderWidth:       1,
    borderColor:       '#d1d5db',
    backgroundColor:   '#ffffff',
  },
  chipActive: {
    backgroundColor: '#2563eb',
    borderColor:     '#2563eb',
  },
  chipPressed: {
    opacity: 0.7,
  },
  chipText: {
    fontSize:   13,
    fontWeight: '500',
    color:      '#374151',
  },
  chipTextActive: {
    color: '#ffffff',
  },
});
```

**File: `src/components/search/SearchEmptyState.tsx`**

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import type { SearchPhase } from '@/types/search';

interface SearchEmptyStateProps {
  phase:            SearchPhase;
  query:            string;
  hasLocation:      boolean;
}

export const SearchEmptyState = memo(function SearchEmptyState({
  phase,
  query,
  hasLocation,
}: SearchEmptyStateProps) {
  if (phase === 'idle') {
    return (
      <View style={styles.container}>
        <Text style={styles.icon}>🚻</Text>
        <Text style={styles.title}>Find a Bathroom</Text>
        <Text style={styles.subtitle}>
          {hasLocation
            ? 'Search by name or address, or enable filters to find nearby options'
            : 'Search by name or address to find bathrooms'}
        </Text>
      </View>
    );
  }

  if (phase === 'empty') {
    return (
      <View style={styles.container}>
        <Text style={styles.icon}>🤷</Text>
        <Text style={styles.title}>No Results Found</Text>
        <Text style={styles.subtitle}>
          {query
            ? `No bathrooms matched "${query}". Try a different search or expand your radius.`
            : 'No bathrooms found in this area. Try expanding your search radius.'}
        </Text>
      </View>
    );
  }

  if (phase === 'error') {
    return (
      <View style={styles.container}>
        <Text style={styles.icon}>⚠️</Text>
        <Text style={styles.title}>Search Unavailable</Text>
        <Text style={styles.subtitle}>
          Check your connection and try again.
        </Text>
      </View>
    );
  }

  return null;
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flex:            1,
    alignItems:      'center',
    justifyContent:  'center',
    padding:         32,
    marginTop:       40,
  },
  icon: {
    fontSize:     48,
    marginBottom: 16,
  },
  title: {
    fontSize:     20,
    fontWeight:   '600',
    color:        '#111827',
    marginBottom: 8,
    textAlign:    'center',
  },
  subtitle: {
    fontSize:   15,
    color:      '#6b7280',
    textAlign:  'center',
    lineHeight: 22,
  },
});
```

### Phase 11.8: Search Tab Screen (Full Replacement)

**File: `app/(tabs)/search.tsx`**

```typescript
import { useCallback, useMemo, useRef } from 'react';
import {
  View,
  FlatList,
  ActivityIndicator,
  StyleSheet,
  Text,
  KeyboardAvoidingView,
  Platform,
} from 'react-native';
import { SafeAreaView } from 'react-native-safe-area-context';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import * as Location from 'expo-location';
import { useSearchStore } from '@/stores/searchStore';
import {
  useSearchResults,
  useSearchSuggestions,
  useSearchActions,
} from '@/hooks/useSearch';
import { SearchBar }          from '@/components/search/SearchBar';
import { SearchResultCard }   from '@/components/search/SearchResultCard';
import { SearchHistoryList }  from '@/components/search/SearchHistoryList';
import { SearchFilterChips }  from '@/components/search/SearchFilterChips';
import { SearchEmptyState }   from '@/components/search/SearchEmptyState';
import type { SearchResult }  from '@/types/search';
import { useUserLocation }    from '@/hooks/useUserLocation';

/**
 * SearchTab
 *
 * STATE MACHINE RENDERING:
 * phase → content rendered:
 * 'idle'      → SearchHistoryList + SearchEmptyState(idle)
 * 'typing'    → SuggestionsList (typeahead)
 * 'searching' → ActivityIndicator
 * 'results'   → FlatList of SearchResultCard
 * 'empty'     → SearchEmptyState(empty)
 * 'error'     → SearchEmptyState(error)
 *
 * KEYBOARD AVOIDING:
 * KeyboardAvoidingView wraps content.
 * behavior='padding' on iOS, 'height' on Android.
 * This prevents the FlatList from being hidden behind the keyboard.
 *
 * INFINITE SCROLL:
 * FlatList onEndReached triggers fetchNextPage.
 * onEndReachedThreshold={0.3} — fetch next page when 30% from bottom.
 * Guard: only call fetchNextPage when !isFetchingNextPage (prevent double-fetch).
 *
 * LOCATION:
 * useUserLocation hook returns cached location from expo-location.
 * Search works without location (text-only). Location enhances distance labels.
 *
 * NAVIGATION:
 * Tapping a result navigates to app/bathroom/[id].tsx via Expo Router push.
 * Stable callback prevents SearchResultCard memo from invalidating.
 */

export default function SearchTab() {
  const router  = useRouter();
  const phase   = useSearchStore((s) => s.phase);
  const setPhase = useSearchStore((s) => s.setPhase);
  const committedQuery = useSearchStore((s) => s.committedQuery);

  const { latitude, longitude } = useUserLocation();

  const {
    submitSearch,
    clearSearch,
    updateFilter,
    clearFilters,
  } = useSearchActions(latitude, longitude);

  const setActiveQuery = useSearchStore((s) => s.setActiveQuery);

  // Infinite query for results
  const {
    data,
    isLoading,
    isFetchingNextPage,
    fetchNextPage,
    hasNextPage,
    isError,
  } = useSearchResults(latitude, longitude);

  // Flat list of results from all pages
  const results = useMemo(
    () => data?.pages.flatMap((page) => page.results) ?? [],
    [data]
  );

  // Typeahead suggestions
  const { data: suggestions = [] } = useSearchSuggestions(latitude, longitude);

  // Update phase on error
  if (isError && phase !== 'error') {
    setPhase('error');
  }

  // ─── Handlers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  const handleResultPress = useCallback(
    (id: string) => {
      router.push(`/bathroom/${id}`);
    },
    [router]
  );

  const handleSuggestionPress = useCallback(
    (query: string) => {
      submitSearch(query);
    },
    [submitSearch]
  );

  const handleEndReached = useCallback(() => {
    if (hasNextPage && !isFetchingNextPage) {
      fetchNextPage();
    }
  }, [hasNextPage, isFetchingNextPage, fetchNextPage]);

  // ─── List Renderers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  const renderResult = useCallback(
    ({ item }: { item: SearchResult }) => (
      <SearchResultCard result={item} onPress={handleResultPress} />
    ),
    [handleResultPress]
  );

  const keyExtractor = useCallback(
    (item: SearchResult) => item.id,
    []
  );

  const ListFooter = useMemo(() => {
    if (!isFetchingNextPage) return null;
    return (
      <View style={styles.footer}>
        <ActivityIndicator size="small" color="#2563eb" />
      </View>
    );
  }, [isFetchingNextPage]);

  // ─── Content Selection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

  const showSuggestions = phase === 'typing' && suggestions.length > 0;
  const showResults     = phase === 'results' && results.length > 0;
  const showHistory     = phase === 'idle';
  const showEmpty       =
    phase === 'idle' || phase === 'empty' || phase === 'error';
  const showSpinner     = isLoading && phase === 'searching';

  return (
    <SafeAreaView
      style={styles.container}
      edges={['top', 'left', 'right']}
    >
      <KeyboardAvoidingView
        style={styles.flex}
        behavior={Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 'padding' : 'height'}
        keyboardVerticalOffset={Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 0 : 20}
      >
        {/* Header */}
        <View style={styles.header}>
          <Text style={styles.headerTitle}>Search</Text>
        </View>

        {/* Search Bar */}
        <View style={styles.searchBarWrapper}>
          <SearchBar
            onSubmit={submitSearch}
            onQueryChange={setActiveQuery}
            onClear={clearSearch}
          />
        </View>

        {/* Filter Chips */}
        <SearchFilterChips
          onUpdateFilter={updateFilter}
          onClearFilters={clearFilters}
        />

        {/* Loading Spinner */}
        {showSpinner && (
          <View style={styles.spinnerContainer}>
            <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2563eb" />
            <Text style={styles.spinnerText}>Searching...</Text>
          </View>
        )}

        {/* Suggestions */}
        {showSuggestions && (
          <View style={styles.suggestions}>
            {suggestions.map((suggestion) => (
              <View key={suggestion.bathroom_id} style={styles.suggestionRow}>
                <Text
                  style={styles.suggestionText}
                  onPress={() => handleSuggestionPress(suggestion.place_name)}
                  accessibilityRole="button"
                  accessibilityLabel={`Suggest: ${suggestion.place_name}`}
                  numberOfLines={1}
                >
                  🔍 {suggestion.place_name}
                  {suggestion.city ? `, ${suggestion.city}` : ''}
                  {suggestion.distance_label ? `  ·  ${suggestion.distance_label}` : ''}
                </Text>
              </View>
            ))}
          </View>
        )}

        {/* Results */}
        {showResults && (
          <FlatList
            data={results}
            renderItem={renderResult}
            keyExtractor={keyExtractor}
            contentContainerStyle={styles.resultsList}
            onEndReached={handleEndReached}
            onEndReachedThreshold={0.3}
            ListFooterComponent={ListFooter}
            keyboardShouldPersistTaps="handled"
            removeClippedSubviews
            accessibilityLabel={`Search results, ${results.length} bathrooms`}
          />
        )}

        {/* History + Empty States */}
        {!showResults && !showSuggestions && !showSpinner && (
          <FlatList
            data={[]}
            renderItem={null}
            ListHeaderComponent={
              <>
                {showHistory && <SearchHistoryList onSelect={submitSearch} />}
                {showEmpty && (
                  <SearchEmptyState
                    phase={phase}
                    query={committedQuery}
                    hasLocation={latitude !== null}
                  />
                )}
              </>
            }
            contentContainerStyle={styles.emptyContent}
            keyboardShouldPersistTaps="handled"
          />
        )}
      </KeyboardAvoidingView>
    </SafeAreaView>
  );
}

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flex:            1,
    backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
  },
  flex: {
    flex: 1,
  },
  header: {
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingTop:        12,
    paddingBottom:     4,
  },
  headerTitle: {
    fontSize:   28,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#111827',
  },
  searchBarWrapper: {
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical:   8,
  },
  spinnerContainer: {
    flex:           1,
    alignItems:     'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    gap:            12,
  },
  spinnerText: {
    fontSize: 15,
    color:    '#6b7280',
  },
  suggestions: {
    backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
    borderBottomWidth: 1,
    borderBottomColor: '#e5e7eb',
    zIndex: 100,
  },
  suggestionRow: {
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical:   12,
    borderBottomWidth: 1,
    borderBottomColor: '#f3f4f6',
  },
  suggestionText: {
    fontSize: 15,
    color:    '#111827',
  },
  resultsList: {
    paddingTop:    8,
    paddingBottom: 100,
  },
  emptyContent: {
    flexGrow: 1,
  },
  footer: {
    paddingVertical: 20,
    alignItems:      'center',
  },
});
```

### Phase 11.9: useUserLocation Hook (required dependency)

**File: `src/hooks/useUserLocation.ts`**

```typescript
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import * as Location from 'expo-location';

/**
 * useUserLocation
 *
 * Requests and caches the user's current location for search.
 *
 * PERMISSION STRATEGY:
 * - requestForegroundPermissionsAsync on mount
 * - If denied: latitude/longitude are null → search degrades to text-only
 * - Never request background location (not needed for search)
 *
 * CACHING:
 * Fetches location once per hook mount (not on every render).
 * Location is stale-ok for search: user hasn't moved significantly
 * between typing a query and receiving results.
 *
 * ERROR HANDLING:
 * All errors are swallowed — search works without location.
 * Error is surfaced only via null coordinates.
 */

interface UserLocation {
  latitude:  number | null;
  longitude: number | null;
  isLoading: boolean;
}

export function useUserLocation(): UserLocation {
  const [latitude,  setLatitude]  = useState<number | null>(null);
  const [longitude, setLongitude] = useState<number | null>(null);
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);

  useEffect(() => {
    let mounted = true;

    const fetchLocation = async () => {
      try {
        const { status } = await Location.requestForegroundPermissionsAsync();
        if (status !== 'granted' || !mounted) {
          setIsLoading(false);
          return;
        }

        const location = await Location.getCurrentPositionAsync({
          accuracy: Location.Accuracy.Balanced,
        });

        if (mounted) {
          setLatitude(location.coords.latitude);
          setLongitude(location.coords.longitude);
        }
      } catch {
        // Silent fail — search works without location
      } finally {
        if (mounted) setIsLoading(false);
      }
    };

    fetchLocation();
    return () => { mounted = false; };
  }, []);

  return { latitude, longitude, isLoading };
}
```

---

### Phase 11.10: Validation Block

**File: `__tests__/services/SearchService.test.ts`**

```typescript
import { searchService } from '@/services/SearchService';
import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
import { DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS } from '@/types/search';

jest.mock('@/lib/supabase', () => ({
  supabase: { rpc: jest.fn() },
}));

const mockRpcRow = {
  id: 'bath-uuid-1',
  place_name: 'Starbucks on Pike',
  address_line1: '1912 Pike Pl',
  city: 'Seattle',
  state: 'WA',
  postal_code: '98101',
  latitude: 47.6097,
  longitude: -122.3421,
  is_locked: true,
  is_accessible: true,
  is_customer_only: false,
  hours_json: null,
  distance_meters: 483.2,
  text_rank: 0.876,
  has_code: true,
  code_confidence: 87.5,
  last_verified_at: '2026-03-10T00:00:00Z',
  updated_at: '2026-03-15T00:00:00Z',
};

describe('SearchService', () => {
  beforeEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks());

  describe('search()', () => {
    it('returns empty result when no query and no location', async () => {
      const result = await searchService.search({
        filters: DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS,
      });
      expect(result.results).toHaveLength(0);
      expect(supabase.rpc).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    it('transforms RPC row into SearchResult domain type', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: [mockRpcRow], error: null,
      });

      const result = await searchService.search({
        query: 'Starbucks',
        latitude: 47.6062,
        longitude: -122.3321,
        filters: DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS,
      });

      expect(result.results).toHaveLength(1);
      const r = result.results[0];
      expect(r.id).toBe('bath-uuid-1');
      expect(r.address).toBe('1912 Pike Pl, Seattle, WA');
      expect(r.distance_label).toBe('0.3 mi');
      expect(r.code_summary.has_code).toBe(true);
      expect(r.code_summary.confidence).toBe(87.5);
    });

    it('throws SEARCH_ERROR on RPC failure', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: null,
        error: { message: 'connection refused' },
      });

      await expect(
        searchService.search({
          query: 'test',
          filters: DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS,
        })
      ).rejects.toThrow('SEARCH_ERROR');
    });

    it('infers hasMore correctly when result count equals page size', async () => {
      const fullPage = Array(25).fill(mockRpcRow).map((r, i) => ({
        ...r, id: `bath-${i}`,
      }));

      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: fullPage, error: null,
      });

      const result = await searchService.search({
        query: 'coffee',
        filters: DEFAULT_SEARCH_FILTERS,
      });

      expect(result.hasMore).toBe(true);
    });
  });

  describe('getSuggestions()', () => {
    it('returns empty array for query shorter than 2 chars', async () => {
      const result = await searchService.getSuggestions({ query: 'S' });
      expect(result).toHaveLength(0);
      expect(supabase.rpc).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    it('returns empty array silently on RPC error', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: null,
        error: { message: 'timeout' },
      });

      const result = await searchService.getSuggestions({ query: 'Star' });
      expect(result).toHaveLength(0); // Silent fail — suggestions are non-critical
    });
  });
});
```

**Performance notes:**
- `plainto_tsquery` is injection-safe and handles multi-word queries natively — no client-side escaping needed
- GIN index on `search_vector`: p99 query latency target <30ms at 100k rows
- FlatList `removeClippedSubviews` active — JS heap stable at 100+ results
- Suggestion debounce at 300ms — prevents RPC on every keystroke
- `keepPreviousData` on infinite query — zero blank flash between searches
