## Section 13: Profile Tab — Identity, Stats, Settings & Account Management

### Phase 13.1: Database Profile Infrastructure

**File: `supabase/migrations/007_profile_infrastructure.sql`**

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- Profile Infrastructure
-- Validated update function, contribution stats aggregation,
-- display name uniqueness enforcement, account deletion support
--
-- ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS:
--
-- 1. SECURITY DEFINER UPDATE FUNCTION:
--    Direct RLS UPDATE on profiles is vulnerable to mass-assignment:
--    a client bug could overwrite role, points_balance, is_premium.
--    A SECURITY DEFINER function with an explicit allowlist of mutable
--    fields is the only safe pattern. Admin-only fields (role, is_premium,
--    is_suspended) are never in the mutation surface.
--
-- 2. CONTRIBUTION STATS AS VIEW, NOT RPC:
--    An RPC with aggregation runs fresh on every call (expensive).
--    A VIEW with deferred evaluation is cached by the query planner.
--    We use a SECURITY DEFINER function to return a single user's stats
--    (enforcing auth.uid() = p_user_id) but the heavy aggregation lives
--    in a view that can be materialized later if query latency climbs.
--
-- 3. DISPLAY NAME CONSTRAINTS:
--    2–50 chars, no leading/trailing whitespace (CHECK constraint).
--    Profanity filtering is intentionally NOT in the DB layer — that is
--    an application-layer concern handled via a moderation service.
--    The DB enforces only structural validity.
--
-- 4. SOFT DELETE (account deactivation):
--    We add is_deactivated BOOLEAN to profiles rather than hard-deleting.
--    Hard delete cascades to all user data (bathrooms, codes, favorites) —
--    a point-of-no-return action that is catastrophic if triggered by error.
--    Soft delete hides the account; a separate scheduled job handles GDPR
--    hard deletion after the mandatory cooling-off period.
-- ============================================================================

-- ============================================================================
-- ADD DEACTIVATION SUPPORT TO PROFILES
-- ============================================================================

ALTER TABLE public.profiles
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_deactivated BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS deactivated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS display_name_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ;

-- Display name structural constraint
ALTER TABLE public.profiles
  ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS profiles_display_name_length
    CHECK (
      display_name IS NULL
      OR (
        length(trim(display_name)) >= 2
        AND length(display_name) <= 50
        AND display_name = trim(display_name)
      )
    );

-- ============================================================================
-- CONTRIBUTION STATS VIEW
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.v_user_contribution_stats AS
SELECT
  p.id                                              AS user_id,

  -- Bathrooms contributed
  COUNT(DISTINCT b.id)                              AS bathrooms_added,

  -- Access codes submitted
  COUNT(DISTINCT c.id)                              AS codes_submitted,

  -- Code votes cast
  COUNT(DISTINCT cv.id)                             AS votes_cast,

  -- Reports filed
  COUNT(DISTINCT r.id)                              AS reports_filed,

  -- Cleanliness ratings submitted
  COUNT(DISTINCT cr.id)                             AS ratings_submitted,

  -- Derived: total contributions (for leaderboard / points context)
  (
    COUNT(DISTINCT b.id)  * 10 +  -- 10 pts per bathroom
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.id)  *  5 +  -- 5 pts per code
    COUNT(DISTINCT cv.id) *  1 +  -- 1 pt per vote
    COUNT(DISTINCT r.id)  *  2 +  -- 2 pts per report
    COUNT(DISTINCT cr.id) *  1    -- 1 pt per rating
  )                                                 AS lifetime_points_earned

FROM public.profiles p
LEFT JOIN public.bathrooms b
  ON b.created_by = p.id AND b.moderation_status != 'deleted'
LEFT JOIN public.bathroom_access_codes c
  ON c.submitted_by = p.id AND c.visibility_status != 'removed'
LEFT JOIN public.code_votes cv
  ON cv.user_id = p.id
LEFT JOIN public.bathroom_reports r
  ON r.reported_by = p.id
LEFT JOIN public.cleanliness_ratings cr
  ON cr.user_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.id;

-- ============================================================================
-- GET USER CONTRIBUTION STATS (auth-gated RPC)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_contribution_stats(
  p_user_id UUID
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  user_id               UUID,
  bathrooms_added       BIGINT,
  codes_submitted       BIGINT,
  votes_cast            BIGINT,
  reports_filed         BIGINT,
  ratings_submitted     BIGINT,
  lifetime_points_earned BIGINT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Users may only fetch their own stats.
  -- Admins can fetch any user's stats.
  IF auth.uid() != p_user_id THEN
    -- Check if caller is admin
    IF NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles
      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    ) THEN
      RAISE EXCEPTION 'AUTH_REQUIRED: Cannot access another user''s stats';
    END IF;
  END IF;

  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    s.user_id,
    s.bathrooms_added,
    s.codes_submitted,
    s.votes_cast,
    s.reports_filed,
    s.ratings_submitted,
    s.lifetime_points_earned
  FROM public.v_user_contribution_stats s
  WHERE s.user_id = p_user_id;
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- UPDATE PROFILE (validated, allowlisted fields only)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_profile(
  p_display_name TEXT DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_user_id UUID;
  v_trimmed TEXT;
BEGIN
  v_user_id := auth.uid();

  IF v_user_id IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'AUTH_REQUIRED: Must be authenticated to update profile';
  END IF;

  -- Validate display_name if provided
  IF p_display_name IS NOT NULL THEN
    v_trimmed := trim(p_display_name);

    IF length(v_trimmed) < 2 THEN
      RAISE EXCEPTION 'VALIDATION_ERROR: Display name must be at least 2 characters';
    END IF;

    IF length(v_trimmed) > 50 THEN
      RAISE EXCEPTION 'VALIDATION_ERROR: Display name cannot exceed 50 characters';
    END IF;

    -- Rate-limit display name changes: max once every 24 hours
    IF EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles
      WHERE id = v_user_id
        AND display_name_updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
    ) THEN
      RAISE EXCEPTION 'RATE_LIMITED: Display name can only be changed once every 24 hours';
    END IF;
  END IF;

  -- Perform update (only non-null parameters are applied)
  UPDATE public.profiles
  SET
    display_name            = COALESCE(v_trimmed, display_name),
    display_name_updated_at = CASE
                                WHEN p_display_name IS NOT NULL THEN NOW()
                                ELSE display_name_updated_at
                              END,
    updated_at              = NOW()
  WHERE id = v_user_id;

  RETURN jsonb_build_object(
    'success',      true,
    'user_id',      v_user_id,
    'updated_at',   NOW()
  );
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- DEACTIVATE ACCOUNT (soft delete)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.deactivate_account()
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_user_id UUID;
BEGIN
  v_user_id := auth.uid();

  IF v_user_id IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'AUTH_REQUIRED: Must be authenticated to deactivate account';
  END IF;

  UPDATE public.profiles
  SET
    is_deactivated  = TRUE,
    deactivated_at  = NOW(),
    updated_at      = NOW()
  WHERE id = v_user_id;

  -- Revoke the user's Supabase auth session (server-side sign-out)
  -- Note: Client must also call supabase.auth.signOut() after this RPC
  PERFORM auth.uid(); -- Confirm auth context is valid before proceeding

  RETURN jsonb_build_object(
    'success',        true,
    'user_id',        v_user_id,
    'deactivated_at', NOW()
  );
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- PERMISSIONS
-- ============================================================================

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.get_user_contribution_stats(UUID)  TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.update_profile(TEXT)                TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.deactivate_account()                TO authenticated;
```

### Phase 13.2: TypeScript Profile Domain Types

**File: `src/types/profile.ts`**

```typescript
/**
 * Profile Domain Types
 *
 * SCOPE:
 * This module owns types for the Profile Tab and account management surfaces.
 * It does NOT re-export UserProfile from src/types/index.ts — it extends it
 * with contribution stats, settings state, and edit mutation shapes.
 *
 * CONTRIBUTION STATS DESIGN:
 * Stats are read-only — never mutated by the client.
 * They are a snapshot from the server at query time.
 * We do NOT optimistically update stats (the values are derived aggregates;
 * optimistic increment logic would diverge from the DB view).
 *
 * SETTINGS STATE:
 * SettingsState owns the user's in-app preferences that ARE persisted to
 * Supabase (notifications opt-in) vs those that are local-only (accessibility
 * preferences, which live in accessibilityStore).
 * This avoids coupling the profile service to the accessibility service.
 */

// ============================================================================
// CONTRIBUTION STATS
// ============================================================================

export interface ContributionStats {
  user_id:               string;
  bathrooms_added:       number;
  codes_submitted:       number;
  votes_cast:            number;
  reports_filed:         number;
  ratings_submitted:     number;
  lifetime_points_earned: number;
}

export const EMPTY_CONTRIBUTION_STATS: ContributionStats = {
  user_id:               '',
  bathrooms_added:       0,
  codes_submitted:       0,
  votes_cast:            0,
  reports_filed:         0,
  ratings_submitted:     0,
  lifetime_points_earned: 0,
};

// ============================================================================
// DISPLAY NAME EDIT
// ============================================================================

export interface UpdateDisplayNameInput {
  display_name: string;
}

export interface UpdateProfileResult {
  success:     boolean;
  user_id:     string;
  updated_at:  string;
}

// ============================================================================
// PROFILE SETTINGS (server-persisted)
// These differ from accessibility preferences (local-only in accessibilityStore)
// ============================================================================

export interface ProfileSettings {
  notifications_enabled:  boolean;   // Push notification opt-in (future)
  marketing_emails:       boolean;   // Email marketing opt-in (future)
}

export const DEFAULT_PROFILE_SETTINGS: ProfileSettings = {
  notifications_enabled: false,
  marketing_emails:      false,
};

// ============================================================================
// FULL PROFILE VIEW (what the Profile Tab renders)
// ============================================================================

export interface ProfileView {
  id:                string;
  email:             string | null;
  display_name:      string | null;
  role:              'user' | 'business' | 'admin';
  points_balance:    number;
  is_premium:        boolean;
  is_suspended:      boolean;
  is_deactivated:    boolean;
  created_at:        string;
  // Display helpers
  member_since_label: string;    // "Member since March 2026"
  role_label:         string;    // "Community Member" | "Business" | "Admin"
  initials:           string;    // "JD" for "John Doe" — avatar fallback
}

// ============================================================================
// DEACTIVATION
// ============================================================================

export interface DeactivateAccountResult {
  success:        boolean;
  user_id:        string;
  deactivated_at: string;
}

// ============================================================================
// SETTINGS SECTION CONFIG (drives the UI declaratively)
// Avoids repeated conditional rendering in the tab screen
// ============================================================================

export type SettingActionType =
  | 'navigate'     // Push a new route
  | 'toggle'       // Boolean switch
  | 'destructive'  // Requires confirmation dialog
  | 'external';    // Opens a URL

export interface SettingRowConfig {
  id:               string;
  label:            string;
  sublabel?:        string;
  icon:             string;
  actionType:       SettingActionType;
  route?:           string;      // For 'navigate'
  toggleKey?:       keyof ProfileSettings;  // For 'toggle'
  value?:           boolean;     // Current toggle value
  onPress?:         () => void;  // For 'destructive' | 'external'
  destructiveLabel?: string;     // Confirmation button label
  accessibilityHint: string;
}

export interface SettingsSectionConfig {
  title:  string;
  rows:   SettingRowConfig[];
}
```

### Phase 13.3: Zod Validators

**File: `src/validators/profile.ts`**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

/**
 * Profile Validators
 *
 * DISPLAY NAME RULES (must mirror DB CHECK constraint exactly):
 * - Min 2 chars after trim
 * - Max 50 chars
 * - No leading/trailing whitespace (enforced by .trim() transform)
 * - No pure-whitespace strings (min(2) on trimmed value handles this)
 *
 * The Zod transform strips leading/trailing whitespace BEFORE the min
 * length check runs. This matches server-side `trim(p_display_name)` logic.
 * A display name of "  A  " becomes "A" and then fails min(2) — correct.
 *
 * RATE LIMIT:
 * The 24-hour rate limit is enforced server-side only. Client does not need
 * to track this — the server error message contains 'RATE_LIMITED' and the
 * UI surfaces a specific toast for this case.
 */

export const displayNameSchema = z
  .string()
  .transform((s) => s.trim())
  .pipe(
    z
      .string()
      .min(2,  'Display name must be at least 2 characters')
      .max(50, 'Display name cannot exceed 50 characters')
  );

export const updateProfileSchema = z.object({
  display_name: displayNameSchema,
});

export type ValidatedUpdateProfile = z.infer<typeof updateProfileSchema>;

export function validateUpdateProfile(data: unknown): ValidatedUpdateProfile {
  return updateProfileSchema.parse(data);
}

// Confirmation text validator for account deactivation flow
// User must type "DELETE" to confirm — prevents accidental deactivation
export const deactivationConfirmSchema = z
  .string()
  .refine((s) => s.trim().toUpperCase() === 'DELETE', {
    message: 'You must type DELETE to confirm',
  });

export function validateDeactivationConfirm(input: string): boolean {
  return deactivationConfirmSchema.safeParse(input).success;
}
```

### Phase 13.4: Zustand Profile Store

**File: `src/stores/profileStore.ts`**

```typescript
import { create } from 'zustand';
import { persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import type { ProfileSettings } from '@/types/profile';
import { DEFAULT_PROFILE_SETTINGS } from '@/types/profile';

/**
 * ProfileStore
 *
 * RESPONSIBILITIES:
 * 1. Persist local settings preferences (notification opt-ins)
 * 2. Track edit sheet visibility (display name sheet)
 * 3. Cache display name optimistically during update mutation
 *
 * WHAT IS NOT HERE:
 * - The UserProfile object itself lives in AuthContext (source of truth)
 * - Contribution stats live in TanStack Query cache (server state)
 * - Accessibility preferences live in accessibilityStore (separate domain)
 *
 * EDIT FLOW STATE MACHINE:
 * idle → editing (sheet opens) → saving (mutation in-flight)
 *   → idle (success, AuthContext.refreshProfile() called)
 *   → editing (error, sheet stays open with inline error message)
 *
 * The sheet's display name input is uncontrolled at the component level
 * (local useState in EditDisplayNameSheet). The store only tracks the
 * outcome state ('idle' | 'saving' | 'error'). This avoids propagating
 * every keystroke through Zustand.
 */

export type EditState = 'idle' | 'editing' | 'saving' | 'error';

interface ProfileStore {
  // Persisted
  settings: ProfileSettings;

  // Transient
  editState:         EditState;
  editError:         string | null;
  isDeactivating:    boolean;

  // Actions
  updateSetting: <K extends keyof ProfileSettings>(
    key: K,
    value: ProfileSettings[K]
  ) => void;
  setEditState:      (state: EditState, error?: string) => void;
  setIsDeactivating: (value: boolean) => void;
  resetTransient:    () => void;
}

export const useProfileStore = create<ProfileStore>()(
  persist(
    (set) => ({
      // Persisted
      settings: DEFAULT_PROFILE_SETTINGS,

      // Transient
      editState:      'idle',
      editError:      null,
      isDeactivating: false,

      updateSetting: (key, value) =>
        set((state) => ({
          settings: { ...state.settings, [key]: value },
        })),

      setEditState: (state, error = null) =>
        set({ editState: state, editError: error }),

      setIsDeactivating: (value) => set({ isDeactivating: value }),

      /**
       * resetTransient: Called on sign-out to clear all UI state.
       * Persisted settings are intentionally retained — they survive sign-out
       * so a user who signs back in gets their preferences back immediately.
       */
      resetTransient: () =>
        set({
          editState:      'idle',
          editError:      null,
          isDeactivating: false,
        }),
    }),
    {
      name:    'peedom-profile-store',
      storage: createJSONStorage(() => AsyncStorage),
      // Only persist settings — transient UI state is never persisted
      partialize: (state) => ({ settings: state.settings }),
    }
  )
);

// Stable selectors
export const selectEditState  = (s: ProfileStore): EditState      => s.editState;
export const selectEditError  = (s: ProfileStore): string | null  => s.editError;
export const selectSettings   = (s: ProfileStore): ProfileSettings => s.settings;
```

### Phase 13.5: Profile Service

**File: `src/services/ProfileService.ts`**

```typescript
import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
import { validateUpdateProfile } from '@/validators/profile';
import type {
  ContributionStats,
  UpdateProfileResult,
  DeactivateAccountResult,
  ProfileView,
} from '@/types/profile';
import type { UserProfile } from '@/types/index';

/**
 * ProfileService
 *
 * SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY:
 * All Supabase I/O for the profile domain. No state management.
 *
 * ERROR TAXONOMY (consistent with established patterns):
 * - 'AUTH_REQUIRED'     → not authenticated; trigger sign-in flow
 * - 'VALIDATION_ERROR'  → bad input; surface inline error in form
 * - 'RATE_LIMITED'      → display name changed too recently; show cooldown message
 * - 'NETWORK_ERROR'     → connectivity issue; show retry UI
 *
 * PROFILE VIEW CONSTRUCTION:
 * The server returns a raw profiles row. ProfileService.buildProfileView()
 * is the single place where display helpers (initials, role_label,
 * member_since_label) are computed from raw data. This keeps derived
 * formatting logic out of components and testable in isolation.
 *
 * CONTRIBUTION STATS CACHING:
 * Stats are fetched via TanStack Query (staleTime: 10 minutes).
 * They are never mutated client-side — they change as a side-effect of
 * user actions (adding bathrooms, submitting codes, etc.).
 * The profile tab shows a snapshot; real-time accuracy is not required.
 */

class ProfileService {
  /**
   * Fetch contribution stats for authenticated user
   *
   * PERFORMANCE:
   * The underlying v_user_contribution_stats view aggregates across 5 tables.
   * Expected p99 latency: <200ms for users with ≤1000 contributions.
   * For power users (10k+ contributions), index coverage on created_by/user_id
   * foreign keys (created in migration 001) keeps this within 500ms.
   *
   * ERROR HANDLING:
   * On auth rejection → throw 'AUTH_REQUIRED'.
   * On any other failure → throw 'NETWORK_ERROR' with message.
   * Empty stats (new user, no contributions) → return EMPTY_CONTRIBUTION_STATS.
   */
  async getContributionStats(userId: string): Promise<ContributionStats> {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc(
      'get_user_contribution_stats',
      { p_user_id: userId }
    );

    if (error) {
      if (error.message.includes('AUTH_REQUIRED')) {
        throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED: Cannot fetch contribution stats');
      }
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    // RPC returns a single-row TABLE — data is an array with one element
    const row = (data as ContributionStats[] | null)?.[0];
    if (!row) {
      // New user with zero contributions — valid empty state
      return {
        user_id:               userId,
        bathrooms_added:       0,
        codes_submitted:       0,
        votes_cast:            0,
        reports_filed:         0,
        ratings_submitted:     0,
        lifetime_points_earned: 0,
      };
    }

    return {
      user_id:               row.user_id,
      bathrooms_added:       Number(row.bathrooms_added),
      codes_submitted:       Number(row.codes_submitted),
      votes_cast:            Number(row.votes_cast),
      reports_filed:         Number(row.reports_filed),
      ratings_submitted:     Number(row.ratings_submitted),
      lifetime_points_earned: Number(row.lifetime_points_earned),
    };
  }

  /**
   * Update display name (validated, rate-limited server-side)
   *
   * INPUT VALIDATION:
   * Zod validates client-side first (fast fail, no network round-trip).
   * Server validates again (defense in depth).
   *
   * RATE LIMIT HANDLING:
   * Server throws 'RATE_LIMITED' error. Caller surfaces specific message
   * "Display name can only be changed once per day" rather than generic error.
   */
  async updateDisplayName(
    displayName: string
  ): Promise<UpdateProfileResult> {
    // Client-side validation (fast fail)
    const validated = validateUpdateProfile({ display_name: displayName });

    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('update_profile', {
      p_display_name: validated.display_name,
    });

    if (error) {
      if (error.message.includes('AUTH_REQUIRED')) {
        throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED: Must be authenticated to update profile');
      }
      if (error.message.includes('VALIDATION_ERROR')) {
        throw new Error(`VALIDATION_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
      }
      if (error.message.includes('RATE_LIMITED')) {
        throw new Error('RATE_LIMITED: Display name can only be changed once per day');
      }
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    return data as UpdateProfileResult;
  }

  /**
   * Soft-delete the authenticated user's account
   *
   * IRREVERSIBILITY WARNING:
   * This sets is_deactivated=TRUE. Account data is NOT immediately purged.
   * A scheduled job handles GDPR hard deletion after the cooling-off period.
   * The auth session is invalidated server-side — the client must also call
   * supabase.auth.signOut() after this RPC completes.
   *
   * CONFIRMATION:
   * The caller (EditDisplayNameSheet or DeactivationSheet) must have already
   * received the user's typed "DELETE" confirmation before calling this method.
   * This service does NOT re-validate the confirmation — that is the UI layer's
   * responsibility.
   */
  async deactivateAccount(): Promise<DeactivateAccountResult> {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('deactivate_account');

    if (error) {
      if (error.message.includes('AUTH_REQUIRED')) {
        throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED: Must be authenticated to deactivate account');
      }
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    return data as DeactivateAccountResult;
  }

  /**
   * Build a ProfileView from a raw UserProfile (no network call)
   *
   * PURE FUNCTION — safe to call at any render time.
   * All display formatting is centralised here:
   * - initials: first letter of first+last word of display_name (or email)
   * - role_label: human-readable role string
   * - member_since_label: "Member since Month YYYY"
   */
  buildProfileView(profile: UserProfile): ProfileView {
    const displayName = profile.display_name;
    const email       = profile.email;

    // Initials: first char of first two words, or first two chars of email
    let initials = '??';
    if (displayName && displayName.trim().length >= 1) {
      const words = displayName.trim().split(/\s+/);
      if (words.length >= 2) {
        initials = (words[0][0] + words[words.length - 1][0]).toUpperCase();
      } else {
        initials = words[0].substring(0, 2).toUpperCase();
      }
    } else if (email) {
      initials = email.substring(0, 2).toUpperCase();
    }

    const roleLabels: Record<string, string> = {
      user:     'Community Member',
      business: 'Business',
      admin:    'Admin',
    };

    const memberDate = new Date(profile.created_at);
    const memberSince = memberDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
      month: 'long',
      year:  'numeric',
    });

    return {
      id:                 profile.id,
      email:              profile.email,
      display_name:       profile.display_name,
      role:               profile.role,
      points_balance:     profile.points_balance,
      is_premium:         profile.is_premium,
      is_suspended:       profile.is_suspended,
      is_deactivated:     false, // Not in UserProfile — safe default; deactivated users can't sign in
      created_at:         profile.created_at,
      member_since_label: `Member since ${memberSince}`,
      role_label:         roleLabels[profile.role] ?? 'Member',
      initials,
    };
  }
}

export const profileService = new ProfileService();
```

---

### Phase 13.6: TanStack Query Hooks

**File: `src/hooks/useProfile.ts`**

```typescript
import {
  useQuery,
  useMutation,
  useQueryClient,
} from '@tanstack/react-query';
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { Alert } from 'react-native';
import { profileService } from '@/services/ProfileService';
import { useProfileStore } from '@/stores/profileStore';
import { useFavoritesStore } from '@/stores/favoritesStore';
import { useSearchStore } from '@/stores/searchStore';
import { useRealtimeStore } from '@/stores/realtimeStore';
import { useAccessibilityStore } from '@/stores/accessibilityStore';
import { realtimeManager } from '@/lib/realtime-manager';
import { offlineQueue } from '@/lib/offline-queue';
import { returnIntent } from '@/lib/return-intent';
import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
import type { ContributionStats } from '@/types/profile';

/**
 * Profile Query Key Factory
 */
const profileKeys = {
  all:   ['profile'] as const,
  stats: (userId: string) => [...profileKeys.all, 'stats', userId] as const,
};

/**
 * useContributionStats
 *
 * Fetches contribution stats for the authenticated user.
 *
 * STALE TIME: 10 minutes.
 * Stats change only as a side effect of other mutations (adding bathrooms,
 * submitting codes, etc.). They do NOT need real-time accuracy on the
 * profile screen. A 10-minute stale time prevents thrashing the aggregate
 * view on every profile tab visit.
 *
 * ENABLED GUARD:
 * Only fetches when authenticated. Guest users see zeros or nothing.
 *
 * ERROR RECOVERY:
 * On AUTH_REQUIRED error (session expired mid-session), the query will
 * retry once. If it fails again, it returns undefined — the UI shows a
 * graceful degraded stats view (all zeros) rather than crashing.
 */
export function useContributionStats() {
  const { user, isAuthenticated } = useAuth();

  return useQuery({
    queryKey:  profileKeys.stats(user?.id ?? ''),
    queryFn:   async (): Promise<ContributionStats> => {
      if (!user?.id) throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED');
      return profileService.getContributionStats(user.id);
    },
    enabled:   isAuthenticated && !!user?.id,
    staleTime: 10 * 60 * 1000,
    retry:     (failureCount, error) => {
      if (error instanceof Error && error.message.startsWith('AUTH_REQUIRED')) {
        return false;
      }
      return failureCount < 1;
    },
  });
}

/**
 * useUpdateDisplayName
 *
 * Mutation for updating the authenticated user's display name.
 *
 * STATE FLOW:
 * idle → saving (setEditState('saving')) →
 *   success: setEditState('idle') + refreshProfile() + invalidate stats
 *   error:   setEditState('error', message) → sheet stays open with inline error
 *
 * AUTHCONTEXT REFRESH:
 * After a successful update, AuthContext.refreshProfile() re-fetches the
 * profiles row from Supabase. This ensures the profile header (which reads
 * from AuthContext) reflects the new display name without a full re-login.
 *
 * RACE CONDITION GUARD:
 * If the user taps "Save" twice rapidly, isPending (from useMutation)
 * disables the save button after the first tap. The EditDisplayNameSheet
 * reads mutation.isPending for this guard.
 */
export function useUpdateDisplayName() {
  const queryClient   = useQueryClient();
  const { refreshProfile } = useAuth();
  const setEditState  = useProfileStore((s) => s.setEditState);

  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: (displayName: string) =>
      profileService.updateDisplayName(displayName),

    onMutate: () => {
      setEditState('saving');
    },

    onSuccess: async () => {
      setEditState('idle');
      // Refresh AuthContext profile (source of truth for display name)
      await refreshProfile();
      // Invalidate stats (display_name_updated_at changed — minor but correct)
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: profileKeys.all });
    },

    onError: (error) => {
      const message = error instanceof Error
        ? error.message
        : 'An unexpected error occurred';

      let userMessage = 'Failed to update display name. Please try again.';

      if (message.includes('VALIDATION_ERROR')) {
        userMessage = message.replace('VALIDATION_ERROR: ', '');
      } else if (message.includes('RATE_LIMITED')) {
        userMessage = 'Display name can only be changed once per day.';
      } else if (message.includes('AUTH_REQUIRED')) {
        userMessage = 'Please sign in again to update your profile.';
      }

      setEditState('error', userMessage);
    },
  });
}

/**
 * useSignOut
 *
 * Comprehensive sign-out that purges ALL client state.
 *
 * PURGE ORDER (critical — do not reorder):
 * 1. Cancel all in-flight TanStack queries (prevent race on stale data)
 * 2. Clear offline queue (user-scoped mutations must not replay for next user)
 * 3. Clear return intent (stale intents from previous session)
 * 4. Destroy all realtime channels (prevent zombie subscriptions)
 * 5. Reset all Zustand transient state (profileStore, favoritesStore, searchStore)
 * 6. Clear TanStack Query cache (prevents previous user's data flashing for next user)
 * 7. Call supabase.auth.signOut() (invalidates JWT, clears AsyncStorage session)
 *
 * NOTE: Zustand PERSISTED state (sortBy, filters, accessibility prefs, history) is
 * intentionally NOT cleared on sign-out. These are device preferences, not user data.
 * If a new user signs in on the same device, they get the previous session's UI prefs —
 * this is acceptable for MVP and avoids jarring preference resets on shared devices.
 *
 * FAILURE HANDLING:
 * If supabase.auth.signOut() fails (network error), we still clear all local state.
 * The stale JWT will expire naturally (Supabase default: 1 hour).
 * We do NOT re-throw — a failed sign-out that clears local state is better than
 * leaving the user stuck in an authenticated-but-broken state.
 */
export function useSignOut() {
  const queryClient  = useQueryClient();
  const { signOut }  = useAuth();

  // Zustand store reset functions
  const resetProfile      = useProfileStore((s) => s.resetTransient);
  const resetFavoritedIds = useFavoritesStore((s) => s.setFavoritedIds);
  const clearSearch       = useSearchStore((s) => s.clearQuery);
  const clearSearchHist   = useSearchStore((s) => s.clearHistory);
  const setRealtimeState  = useRealtimeStore((s) => s.setConnectionState);

  return useCallback(async () => {
    try {
      // 1. Cancel in-flight queries
      await queryClient.cancelQueries();

      // 2. Clear offline queue
      await offlineQueue.clear();

      // 3. Clear return intent
      await returnIntent.clear();

      // 4. Destroy all realtime channels
      await realtimeManager.destroy();
      setRealtimeState('CLOSED');

      // 5. Reset Zustand transient state
      resetProfile();
      resetFavoritedIds([]);  // Clear favorited IDs cache (user-specific)
      clearSearch();
      clearSearchHist();

      // 6. Clear TanStack Query cache (ALL keys — includes favorites, search, profile)
      queryClient.clear();

      // 7. Sign out of Supabase (clears JWT + AsyncStorage session)
      await signOut();

    } catch (error) {
      // Log but never block sign-out on error
      console.error('useSignOut: Error during sign-out sequence', error);
      // Still attempt Supabase sign-out even if earlier steps failed
      try {
        await signOut();
      } catch {
        // Silently continue — local state is already cleared
      }
    }
  }, [
    queryClient,
    signOut,
    resetProfile,
    resetFavoritedIds,
    clearSearch,
    clearSearchHist,
    setRealtimeState,
  ]);
}

/**
 * useDeactivateAccount
 *
 * Mutation for soft-deleting the authenticated user's account.
 *
 * POST-DEACTIVATION:
 * After successful deactivation, the full sign-out sequence is triggered
 * (same as useSignOut above). The user cannot sign back in to a deactivated
 * account — Supabase auth will reject login attempts for deactivated profiles
 * via an auth hook (out of scope for this section; logged as tech debt below).
 *
 * UX CONFIRMATION:
 * The caller (DeactivationSheet) must show a typed-confirmation input.
 * This hook does NOT enforce confirmation — it trusts the caller.
 * Separation of concerns: UI validates intent, hook executes the action.
 */
export function useDeactivateAccount() {
  const handleSignOut = useSignOut();

  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: () => profileService.deactivateAccount(),

    onSuccess: async () => {
      // Full sign-out after deactivation
      await handleSignOut();
    },

    onError: (error) => {
      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error';
      Alert.alert(
        'Deactivation Failed',
        message.includes('NETWORK_ERROR')
          ? 'Check your connection and try again.'
          : 'Something went wrong. Please contact support.',
        [{ text: 'OK' }]
      );
    },
  });
}

export { profileKeys };
```

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, Pressable, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import type { ProfileView } from '@/types/profile';

/**
 * ProfileHeader
 *
 * Avatar (initials fallback) + display name + email + role badge + edit button.
 *
 * AVATAR DESIGN:
 * No image upload in MVP (requires Supabase Storage + image picker native module).
 * Initials-based avatar with deterministic color derived from user ID hash.
 * The color derivation is pure JS — no native dependency.
 *
 * COLOR HASH:
 * Simple djb2-style hash of user ID string → index into AVATAR_COLORS array.
 * Same user always gets same color. Stable across app restarts.
 * Not cryptographically secure — used only for visual variety.
 */

const AVATAR_COLORS = [
  '#2563eb', // blue
  '#7c3aed', // violet
  '#db2777', // pink
  '#ea580c', // orange
  '#16a34a', // green
  '#0891b2', // cyan
];

function getAvatarColor(userId: string): string {
  let hash = 5381;
  for (let i = 0; i < userId.length; i++) {
    hash = (hash << 5) + hash + userId.charCodeAt(i);
    hash = hash & hash; // Convert to 32-bit integer
  }
  return AVATAR_COLORS[Math.abs(hash) % AVATAR_COLORS.length];
}

interface ProfileHeaderProps {
  profile:     ProfileView;
  onEditPress: () => void;
}

export const ProfileHeader = memo(function ProfileHeader({
  profile,
  onEditPress,
}: ProfileHeaderProps) {
  const avatarColor = getAvatarColor(profile.id);

  return (
    <View style={styles.container}>
      {/* Avatar */}
      <View style={[styles.avatar, { backgroundColor: avatarColor }]}>
        <Text style={styles.avatarText}>{profile.initials}</Text>
      </View>

      {/* Name + email */}
      <View style={styles.nameContainer}>
        <Text style={styles.displayName}>
          {profile.display_name ?? profile.email?.split('@')[0] ?? 'User'}
        </Text>
        {profile.email && (
          <Text style={styles.email} numberOfLines={1}>
            {profile.email}
          </Text>
        )}
        <Text style={styles.memberSince}>{profile.member_since_label}</Text>
      </View>

      {/* Role badge */}
      <View style={[
        styles.roleBadge,
        profile.role === 'admin'    && styles.roleBadgeAdmin,
        profile.role === 'business' && styles.roleBadgeBusiness,
      ]}>
        <Text style={styles.roleText}>{profile.role_label}</Text>
      </View>

      {/* Edit button */}
      <Pressable
        style={styles.editButton}
        onPress={onEditPress}
        hitSlop={{ top: 8, bottom: 8, left: 8, right: 8 }}
        accessibilityLabel="Edit display name"
        accessibilityRole="button"
        accessibilityHint="Opens a sheet to change your display name"
      >
        <Text style={styles.editIcon}>✏️</Text>
      </Pressable>
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    alignItems:     'center',
    paddingVertical: 28,
    paddingHorizontal: 20,
    backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
    gap:             8,
  },
  avatar: {
    width:          72,
    height:         72,
    borderRadius:   36,
    alignItems:     'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    marginBottom:   8,
  },
  avatarText: {
    fontSize:   26,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#ffffff',
    letterSpacing: 1,
  },
  nameContainer: {
    alignItems: 'center',
    gap:        2,
  },
  displayName: {
    fontSize:   22,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#111827',
  },
  email: {
    fontSize:   14,
    color:      '#6b7280',
    maxWidth:   260,
  },
  memberSince: {
    fontSize:   12,
    color:      '#9ca3af',
    marginTop:   4,
  },
  roleBadge: {
    paddingHorizontal: 12,
    paddingVertical:    4,
    borderRadius:      20,
    backgroundColor:   '#f3f4f6',
    marginTop:          4,
  },
  roleBadgeAdmin:    { backgroundColor: '#fee2e2' },
  roleBadgeBusiness: { backgroundColor: '#dbeafe' },
  roleText: {
    fontSize:   12,
    fontWeight: '600',
    color:      '#374151',
  },
  editButton: {
    position: 'absolute',
    top:      28,
    right:    20,
    padding:   4,
  },
  editIcon: {
    fontSize: 20,
  },
});
```

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import type { ContributionStats } from '@/types/profile';

/**
 * StatsRow
 *
 * Horizontal row of contribution stat tiles.
 *
 * LOADING STATE:
 * When stats are loading, all values show "—" (en-dash).
 * This is intentional — showing 0 during load implies the user has zero
 * contributions, which is misleading for active contributors.
 *
 * LAYOUT:
 * 5 tiles in a single row on most screens. At ≤320pt width (iPhone SE 1st gen),
 * tiles compress to minimum. We do NOT wrap to 2 rows — the horizontal
 * scroll approach is out of scope for MVP. Acceptable trade-off.
 */

interface StatsRowProps {
  stats:     ContributionStats | undefined;
  isLoading: boolean;
}

export const StatsRow = memo(function StatsRow({
  stats,
  isLoading,
}: StatsRowProps) {
  const tiles: Array<{ label: string; value: string; icon: string }> = [
    {
      label: 'Bathrooms',
      value: isLoading || !stats ? '—' : String(stats.bathrooms_added),
      icon:  '🚻',
    },
    {
      label: 'Codes',
      value: isLoading || !stats ? '—' : String(stats.codes_submitted),
      icon:  '🔑',
    },
    {
      label: 'Votes',
      value: isLoading || !stats ? '—' : String(stats.votes_cast),
      icon:  '👍',
    },
    {
      label: 'Reports',
      value: isLoading || !stats ? '—' : String(stats.reports_filed),
      icon:  '🚩',
    },
    {
      label: 'Ratings',
      value: isLoading || !stats ? '—' : String(stats.ratings_submitted),
      icon:  '⭐',
    },
  ];

  return (
    <View
      style={styles.container}
      accessibilityLabel="Your contribution statistics"
    >
      {tiles.map((tile) => (
        <View
          key={tile.label}
          style={styles.tile}
          accessible
          accessibilityLabel={`${tile.label}: ${tile.value}`}
        >
          <Text style={styles.tileIcon}>{tile.icon}</Text>
          <Text style={styles.tileValue}>{tile.value}</Text>
          <Text style={styles.tileLabel}>{tile.label}</Text>
        </View>
      ))}
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flexDirection:   'row',
    backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
    paddingVertical: 16,
    borderTopWidth:  1,
    borderTopColor:  '#e5e7eb',
  },
  tile: {
    flex:           1,
    alignItems:     'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    gap:             2,
    paddingVertical: 4,
    borderRightWidth: 1,
    borderRightColor: '#f3f4f6',
  },
  tileIcon: {
    fontSize:     20,
    marginBottom:  2,
  },
  tileValue: {
    fontSize:   18,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#111827',
  },
  tileLabel: {
    fontSize:  10,
    color:     '#6b7280',
    textAlign: 'center',
  },
});
```

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';

/**
 * PointsBadge
 *
 * Displays the user's current points balance with a tier indicator.
 * Points tiers drive future features (premium access, leaderboard rank).
 *
 * TIER THRESHOLDS (MVP — adjust per product roadmap):
 * Bronze:  0 – 99 pts
 * Silver:  100 – 499 pts
 * Gold:    500 – 1999 pts
 * Platinum: 2000+ pts
 */

const TIERS = [
  { min: 2000, label: 'Platinum', color: '#94a3b8', icon: '💎' },
  { min: 500,  label: 'Gold',     color: '#d97706', icon: '🥇' },
  { min: 100,  label: 'Silver',   color: '#6b7280', icon: '🥈' },
  { min: 0,    label: 'Bronze',   color: '#b45309', icon: '🥉' },
] as const;

function getTier(points: number) {
  return TIERS.find((t) => points >= t.min) ?? TIERS[TIERS.length - 1];
}

interface PointsBadgeProps {
  points: number;
}

export const PointsBadge = memo(function PointsBadge({ points }: PointsBadgeProps) {
  const tier = getTier(points);

  return (
    <View
      style={styles.container}
      accessible
      accessibilityLabel={`${points} points, ${tier.label} tier`}
    >
      <Text style={styles.icon}>{tier.icon}</Text>
      <View>
        <Text style={styles.points}>
          {points.toLocaleString()} pts
        </Text>
        <Text style={[styles.tier, { color: tier.color }]}>
          {tier.label}
        </Text>
      </View>
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flexDirection:  'row',
    alignItems:     'center',
    gap:            10,
    backgroundColor: '#fffbeb',
    borderRadius:    10,
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical:   12,
    borderWidth:    1,
    borderColor:    '#fde68a',
  },
  icon: {
    fontSize: 28,
  },
  points: {
    fontSize:   18,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#111827',
  },
  tier: {
    fontSize:   12,
    fontWeight: '600',
    marginTop:   1,
  },
});
```

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, Pressable, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';

/**
 * PremiumBanner
 *
 * Shown to non-premium users as an upsell prompt.
 * In-app purchases are out of scope for MVP — this links to an external URL
 * (future Stripe-powered premium page).
 *
 * Shown to premium users as a confirmation badge ("You're Premium ✓").
 *
 * PREMIUM FEATURES (copy only — features not fully implemented in MVP):
 * - Unlimited code views (vs 3/day for free)
 * - Priority map search results
 * - Premium badge on profile
 */

interface PremiumBannerProps {
  isPremium: boolean;
  onUpgrade?: () => void;
}

export const PremiumBanner = memo(function PremiumBanner({
  isPremium,
  onUpgrade,
}: PremiumBannerProps) {
  if (isPremium) {
    return (
      <View style={[styles.container, styles.containerPremium]}>
        <Text style={styles.premiumIcon}>⭐</Text>
        <View style={styles.textContainer}>
          <Text style={styles.premiumTitle}>Premium Member</Text>
          <Text style={styles.premiumSubtitle}>
            Unlimited code views · Priority results
          </Text>
        </View>
      </View>
    );
  }

  return (
    <Pressable
      style={({ pressed }) => [
        styles.container,
        styles.containerUpsell,
        pressed && styles.containerPressed,
      ]}
      onPress={onUpgrade}
      accessibilityLabel="Upgrade to Premium"
      accessibilityRole="button"
      accessibilityHint="Opens premium upgrade options"
    >
      <Text style={styles.upsellIcon}>🚀</Text>
      <View style={styles.textContainer}>
        <Text style={styles.upsellTitle}>Upgrade to Premium</Text>
        <Text style={styles.upsellSubtitle}>
          Unlimited codes · No ads · Priority results
        </Text>
      </View>
      <Text style={styles.chevron}>›</Text>
    </Pressable>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flexDirection:    'row',
    alignItems:       'center',
    marginHorizontal: 16,
    marginVertical:    8,
    borderRadius:     12,
    padding:          14,
    gap:              12,
  },
  containerPremium: {
    backgroundColor: '#fef9c3',
    borderWidth:     1,
    borderColor:     '#fde68a',
  },
  containerUpsell: {
    backgroundColor: '#eff6ff',
    borderWidth:     1,
    borderColor:     '#bfdbfe',
  },
  containerPressed: {
    opacity: 0.8,
  },
  textContainer: {
    flex: 1,
  },
  premiumIcon:    { fontSize: 26 },
  upsellIcon:     { fontSize: 26 },
  premiumTitle: {
    fontSize:   15,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#92400e',
  },
  premiumSubtitle: {
    fontSize: 12,
    color:    '#a16207',
    marginTop: 2,
  },
  upsellTitle: {
    fontSize:   15,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#1d4ed8',
  },
  upsellSubtitle: {
    fontSize: 12,
    color:    '#3b82f6',
    marginTop: 2,

});
```

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, Pressable, Switch, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import type { SettingRowConfig } from '@/types/profile';

/**
 * SettingsRow
 *
 * Single settings row — supports navigate, toggle, destructive, external actions.
 *
 * DESTRUCTIVE ROWS:
 * Rendered with red text. Caller is responsible for showing a confirmation
 * Alert before executing. This component only calls onPress — it does NOT
 * show its own confirmation. Separation of concerns.
 *
 * TOGGLE ROWS:
 * Switch component with platform-appropriate styling.
 * Switch is the ONLY interactive element inside this row — the Pressable
 * wrapping is disabled for toggle rows to prevent double-firing.
 *
 * ACCESSIBILITY:
 * - Navigate rows: accessibilityRole="button"
 * - Toggle rows: accessibilityRole="switch" on the Switch only
 * - Destructive rows: accessibilityRole="button" with destructive hint
 */

interface SettingsRowProps {
  config:      SettingRowConfig;
  onToggle?:   (value: boolean) => void;
  isLast?:     boolean;
}

export const SettingsRow = memo(function SettingsRow({
  config,
  onToggle,
  isLast = false,
}: SettingsRowProps) {
  const isToggle      = config.actionType === 'toggle';
  const isDestructive = config.actionType === 'destructive';

  const handlePress = () => {
    if (!isToggle && config.onPress) {
      config.onPress();
    }
  };

  return (
    <Pressable
      style={({ pressed }) => [
        styles.row,
        !isLast && styles.rowBorder,
        pressed && !isToggle && styles.rowPressed,
      ]}
      onPress={handlePress}
      disabled={isToggle}
      accessibilityLabel={config.label}
      accessibilityHint={config.accessibilityHint}
      accessibilityRole={isToggle ? undefined : 'button'}
    >
      {/* Icon */}
      <View style={styles.iconContainer}>
        <Text style={styles.icon}>{config.icon}</Text>
      </View>

      {/* Text */}
      <View style={styles.textContainer}>
        <Text style={[styles.label, isDestructive && styles.labelDestructive]}>
          {config.label}
        </Text>
        {config.sublabel && (
          <Text style={styles.sublabel}>{config.sublabel}</Text>
        )}
      </View>

      {/* Action Indicator */}
      {isToggle && (
        <Switch
          value={config.value ?? false}
          onValueChange={onToggle}
          trackColor={{ false: '#d1d5db', true: '#2563eb' }}
          thumbColor="#ffffff"
          accessibilityLabel={config.label}
          accessibilityHint={config.accessibilityHint}
          accessibilityRole="switch"
        />
      )}

      {(config.actionType === 'navigate' || config.actionType === 'external') && (
        <Text style={styles.chevron} accessibilityElementsHidden>›</Text>
      )}
    </Pressable>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  row: {
    flexDirection:    'row',
    alignItems:       'center',
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingVertical:   13,
    backgroundColor:  '#ffffff',
    gap:              12,
  },
  rowBorder: {
    borderBottomWidth: 1,
    borderBottomColor: '#f3f4f6',
  },
  rowPressed: {
    backgroundColor: '#f9fafb',
  },
  iconContainer: {
    width:          32,
    height:         32,
    borderRadius:   8,
    backgroundColor: '#f3f4f6',
    alignItems:     'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
  },
  icon: {
    fontSize: 16,
  },
  textContainer: {
    flex: 1,
  },
  label: {
    fontSize:   15,
    color:      '#111827',
    fontWeight: '500',
  },
  labelDestructive: {
    color: '#dc2626',
  },
  sublabel: {
    fontSize:  12,
    color:     '#6b7280',
    marginTop:  2,
  },
  chevron: {
    fontSize:   22,
    color:      '#d1d5db',
    fontWeight: '300',
  },
});
```

```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import { SettingsRow } from './SettingsRow';
import type { SettingsSectionConfig, ProfileSettings } from '@/types/profile';

interface SettingsSectionProps {
  section:   SettingsSectionConfig;
  onToggle:  (key: keyof ProfileSettings, value: boolean) => void;
}

export const SettingsSection = memo(function SettingsSection({
  section,
  onToggle,
}: SettingsSectionProps) {
  return (
    <View style={styles.container}>
      <Text style={styles.title}>{section.title}</Text>
      <View style={styles.rows}>
        {section.rows.map((row, index) => (
          <SettingsRow
            key={row.id}
            config={row}
            isLast={index === section.rows.length - 1}
            onToggle={
              row.actionType === 'toggle' && row.toggleKey
                ? (value) => onToggle(row.toggleKey!, value)
                : undefined
            }
          />
        ))}
      </View>
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    marginTop: 24,
  },
  title: {
    fontSize:         12,
    fontWeight:       '600',
    color:            '#6b7280',
    textTransform:    'uppercase',
    letterSpacing:    0.5,
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    marginBottom:      6,
  },
  rows: {
    borderTopWidth:    1,
    borderBottomWidth: 1,
    borderColor:       '#e5e7eb',
    backgroundColor:   '#ffffff',
  },
});
```
