```typescript
import { memo } from 'react';
import { View, Text, Pressable, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';

/**
 * GuestProfileView
 *
 * Shown when no authenticated session exists.
 * Provides sign-in CTA without exposing any profile data.
 *
 * DESIGN CHOICE:
 * We show a stripped-down "anonymous" avatar + sign-in prompt rather
 * than an empty screen. The user understands they have a profile waiting
 * once they sign in. This increases sign-in conversion vs a blank tab.
 */

export const GuestProfileView = memo(function GuestProfileView() {
  const router = useRouter();

  return (
    <View style={styles.container}>
      {/* Anonymous Avatar */}
      <View style={styles.avatar}>
        <Text style={styles.avatarText}>?</Text>
      </View>

      <Text style={styles.title}>You're browsing as a guest</Text>
      <Text style={styles.subtitle}>
        Sign in to track your contributions, save favorites, and manage your profile.
      </Text>

      <View style={styles.buttons}>
        <Pressable
          style={styles.primaryButton}
          onPress={() => router.push('/(auth)/login')}
          accessibilityLabel="Sign in to your account"
          accessibilityRole="button"
        >
          <Text style={styles.primaryButtonText}>Sign In</Text>
        </Pressable>

        <Pressable
          style={styles.secondaryButton}
          onPress={() => router.push('/(auth)/register')}
          accessibilityLabel="Create a new account"
          accessibilityRole="button"
        >
          <Text style={styles.secondaryButtonText}>Create Account</Text>
        </Pressable>
      </View>

      {/* Feature preview */}
      <View style={styles.featureList}>
        {[
          { icon: '🚻', text: 'Add bathrooms to the community map' },
          { icon: '♥',  text: 'Save your favorite bathrooms' },
          { icon: '🔑', text: 'Submit and verify access codes' },
          { icon: '⭐', text: 'Earn points for contributions' },
        ].map((f) => (
          <View key={f.text} style={styles.featureRow}>
            <Text style={styles.featureIcon}>{f.icon}</Text>
            <Text style={styles.featureText}>{f.text}</Text>
          </View>
        ))}
      </View>
    </View>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flex:           1,
    alignItems:     'center',
    padding:        28,
    paddingTop:     40,
  },
  avatar: {
    width:          72,
    height:         72,
    borderRadius:   36,
    backgroundColor: '#e5e7eb',
    alignItems:     'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
    marginBottom:   16,
  },
  avatarText: {
    fontSize:   32,
    color:      '#9ca3af',
    fontWeight: '300',
  },
  title: {
    fontSize:   20,
    fontWeight: '700',
    color:      '#111827',
    marginBottom: 8,
    textAlign:  'center',
  },
  subtitle: {
    fontSize:     14,
    color:        '#6b7280',
    textAlign:    'center',
    lineHeight:   20,
    marginBottom: 24,
  },
  buttons: {
    width: '100%',
    gap:    10,
    marginBottom: 32,
  },
  primaryButton: {
    backgroundColor: '#2563eb',
    borderRadius:    10,
    paddingVertical: 14,
    alignItems:      'center',
  },
  primaryButtonText: {
    color:      '#ffffff',
    fontSize:   16,
    fontWeight: '600',
  },
  secondaryButton: {
    borderWidth:     1,
    borderColor:     '#d1d5db',
    borderRadius:    10,
    paddingVertical: 14,
    alignItems:      'center',
    backgroundColor: '#ffffff',
  },
  secondaryButtonText: {
    color:    '#374151',
    fontSize: 16,
  },
  featureList: {
    width:     '100%',
    gap:        10,
  },
  featureRow: {
    flexDirection: 'row',
    alignItems:    'center',
    gap:           10,
  },
  featureIcon: { fontSize: 18, width: 24, textAlign: 'center' },
  featureText: { fontSize: 14, color: '#6b7280' },
});
```

**File: `src/components/profile/EditDisplayNameSheet.tsx`**

```typescript
import { memo, useRef, useState, useCallback } from 'react';
import {
  View,
  Text,
  TextInput,
  Pressable,
  StyleSheet,
  KeyboardAvoidingView,
  Platform,
  ActivityIndicator,
  Alert,
} from 'react-native';
import BottomSheet, { BottomSheetView } from '@gorhom/bottom-sheet';
import { useUpdateDisplayName } from '@/hooks/useProfile';
import { useProfileStore } from '@/stores/profileStore';
import { validateDeactivationConfirm } from '@/validators/profile';

/**
 * EditDisplayNameSheet
 *
 * Bottom sheet for editing display name.
 * Separate from deactivation — we do NOT combine these into one sheet
 * (single responsibility; deactivation is a destructive action with
 * its own confirmation flow).
 *
 * INPUT HANDLING:
 * - Local state (useState) for the text value — NOT Zustand
 * - Zustand stores only editState and editError (from profileStore)
 * - This separation means typing does NOT trigger global re-renders
 *
 * SAVE BUTTON STATE:
 * - Disabled when: mutation.isPending OR value is empty OR value matches current name
 * - Shows ActivityIndicator when saving
 * - Text reverts to "Save" on error (sheet stays open, error shown inline)
 *
 * KEYBOARD HANDLING:
 * KeyboardAvoidingView with behavior='padding' (iOS) or 'height' (Android).
 * TextInput gets autoFocus when sheet opens — user can start typing immediately.
 *
 * SNAP POINT:
 * Single snap point: '40%' — just enough for the input, buttons, and keyboard.
 * enablePanDownToClose={false} — prevents accidental dismissal while typing.
 * Closed by explicit Cancel button or successful save only.
 */

interface EditDisplayNameSheetProps {
  currentDisplayName: string | null;
  isOpen:             boolean;
  onClose:            () => void;
}

export const EditDisplayNameSheet = memo(function EditDisplayNameSheet({
  currentDisplayName,
  isOpen,
  onClose,
}: EditDisplayNameSheetProps) {
  const bottomSheetRef = useRef<BottomSheet>(null);
  const [value, setValue] = useState(currentDisplayName ?? '');

  const editState = useProfileStore((s) => s.editState);
  const editError = useProfileStore((s) => s.editError);

  const { mutate: updateDisplayName, isPending } = useUpdateDisplayName();

  const isSaveDisabled =
    isPending ||
    value.trim().length < 2 ||
    value.trim() === currentDisplayName;

  const handleSave = useCallback(() => {
    if (isSaveDisabled) return;
    updateDisplayName(value, {
      onSuccess: () => onClose(),
    });
  }, [value, isSaveDisabled, updateDisplayName, onClose]);

  const handleCancel = useCallback(() => {
    setValue(currentDisplayName ?? '');
    onClose();
  }, [currentDisplayName, onClose]);

  if (!isOpen) return null;

  return (
    <BottomSheet
      ref={bottomSheetRef}
      index={0}
      snapPoints={['42%']}
      enablePanDownToClose={false}
      backgroundStyle={styles.background}
      handleIndicatorStyle={styles.handle}
      accessibilityViewIsModal
      accessibilityLabel="Edit display name"
    >
      <BottomSheetView style={styles.content}>
        <KeyboardAvoidingView
          behavior={Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 'padding' : 'height'}
          style={styles.flex}
        >
          {/* Header */}
          <View style={styles.header}>
            <Pressable
              onPress={handleCancel}
              disabled={isPending}
              accessibilityRole="button"
              accessibilityLabel="Cancel editing display name"
            >
              <Text style={[styles.headerAction, isPending && styles.headerActionDisabled]}>
                Cancel
              </Text>
            </Pressable>
            <Text style={styles.headerTitle}>Display Name</Text>
            <Pressable
              onPress={handleSave}
              disabled={isSaveDisabled}
              accessibilityRole="button"
              accessibilityLabel="Save display name"
              accessibilityState={{ disabled: isSaveDisabled }}
            >
              {isPending ? (
                <ActivityIndicator size="small" color="#2563eb" />
              ) : (
                <Text style={[
                  styles.headerAction,
                  styles.headerActionSave,
                  isSaveDisabled && styles.headerActionDisabled,
                ]}>
                  Save
                </Text>
              )}
            </Pressable>
          </View>

          {/* Input */}
          <View style={styles.inputContainer}>
            <TextInput
              style={styles.input}
              value={value}
              onChangeText={setValue}
              onSubmitEditing={handleSave}
              placeholder="Enter display name"
              placeholderTextColor="#9ca3af"
              autoFocus
              maxLength={50}
              returnKeyType="done"
              autoCapitalize="words"
              autoCorrect={false}
              accessibilityLabel="Display name input"
              accessibilityHint="Enter 2 to 50 characters for your display name"
            />
            <Text style={styles.charCount}>
              {value.trim().length}/50
            </Text>
          </View>

          {/* Inline error */}
          {editState === 'error' && editError && (
            <View style={styles.errorContainer}>
              <Text style={styles.errorText}>⚠️ {editError}</Text>
            </View>
          )}

          <Text style={styles.hint}>
            Display names must be 2–50 characters. You can change this once per day.
          </Text>
        </KeyboardAvoidingView>
      </BottomSheetView>
    </BottomSheet>
  );
});

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  flex:       { flex: 1 },
  background: { backgroundColor: '#ffffff', borderTopLeftRadius: 16, borderTopRightRadius: 16 },
  handle:     { backgroundColor: '#d1d5db', width: 40 },
  content: {
    flex:    1,
    padding: 20,
  },
  header: {
    flexDirection:  'row',
    justifyContent: 'space-between',
    alignItems:     'center',
    marginBottom:   20,
  },
  headerTitle: {
    fontSize:   16,
    fontWeight: '600',
    color:      '#111827',
  },
  headerAction: {
    fontSize:   16,
    color:      '#6b7280',
  },
  headerActionSave: {
    color:      '#2563eb',
    fontWeight: '600',
  },
  headerActionDisabled: {
    opacity: 0.4,
  },
  inputContainer: {
    borderWidth:     1,
    borderColor:     '#d1d5db',
    borderRadius:    10,
    paddingHorizontal: 14,
    paddingVertical:    4,
    flexDirection:   'row',
    alignItems:      'center',
    backgroundColor: '#f9fafb',
    marginBottom:    8,
  },
  input: {
    flex:     1,
    fontSize: 16,
    color:    '#111827',
    paddingVertical: 10,
  },
  charCount: {
    fontSize: 12,
    color:    '#9ca3af',
  },
  errorContainer: {
    backgroundColor: '#fee2e2',
    borderRadius:    8,
    padding:         10,
    marginBottom:    8,
  },
  errorText: {
    fontSize: 13,
    color:    '#dc2626',
  },
  hint: {
    fontSize:  12,
    color:     '#9ca3af',
    lineHeight: 18,
  },
});
```

---

### Phase 13.8: Profile Tab Screen (Full Replacement)

**File: `app/(tabs)/profile.tsx`**

```typescript
import { useState, useCallback, useMemo } from 'react';
import {
  View,
  ScrollView,
  StyleSheet,
  Alert,
  Linking,
} from 'react-native';
import { SafeAreaView } from 'react-native-safe-area-context';
import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
import {
  useContributionStats,
  useSignOut,
  useDeactivateAccount,
} from '@/hooks/useProfile';
import { useProfileStore } from '@/stores/profileStore';
import { profileService } from '@/services/ProfileService';
import { GuestProfileView }       from '@/components/profile/GuestProfileView';
import { ProfileHeader }          from '@/components/profile/ProfileHeader';
import { StatsRow }               from '@/components/profile/StatsRow';
import { PointsBadge }            from '@/components/profile/PointsBadge';
import { PremiumBanner }          from '@/components/profile/PremiumBanner';
import { SettingsSection }        from '@/components/profile/SettingsSection';
import { EditDisplayNameSheet }   from '@/components/profile/EditDisplayNameSheet';
import type {
  ProfileSettings,
  SettingsSectionConfig,
} from '@/types/profile';

/**
 * ProfileTab
 *
 * RENDERING STATES:
 * 1. GUEST → GuestProfileView (sign-in/register CTAs)
 * 2. AUTHENTICATED → Full profile with header, stats, points, settings
 *
 * SETTINGS SECTIONS (declarative config):
 * We define settings as a data structure rather than JSX trees.
 * This makes the settings list easy to extend, reorder, and test
 * without modifying component rendering logic.
 *
 * SIGN-OUT CONFIRMATION:
 * Alert.alert() is the React Native idiomatic confirmation pattern.
 * We do NOT use a bottom sheet for sign-out confirmation — it is a
 * single-action confirmation, not a complex flow.
 *
 * DEACTIVATION FLOW:
 * Multi-step: Alert warning → type "DELETE" confirmation (future modal).
 * For MVP: Alert with two-step confirmation (simpler, ships faster).
 * The typed confirmation (deactivationConfirmSchema) is enforced in the
 * full implementation. For MVP we use Alert buttons only.
 *
 * EDIT SHEET LIFECYCLE:
 * isEditSheetOpen is local component state — NOT in Zustand.
 * Zustand only holds editState (idle/saving/error) and editError (string).
 * The sheet open/close state is purely a UI concern, not domain state.
 *
 * SCROLL VIEW (not FlatList):
 * Settings are a fixed list of O(10) items — FlatList is overkill.
 * ScrollView with contentContainerStyle is appropriate here.
 * Profile header, stats row, points badge, premium banner, and settings
 * all render in one pass — no virtualization needed.
 */

export default function ProfileTab() {
  const { profile, isAuthenticated } = useAuth();
  const [isEditSheetOpen, setIsEditSheetOpen] = useState(false);

  const settings     = useProfileStore((s) => s.settings);
  const updateSetting = useProfileStore((s) => s.updateSetting);

  const { data: stats, isLoading: statsLoading } = useContributionStats();

  const handleSignOut     = useSignOut();
  const { mutate: deactivate, isPending: isDeactivating } = useDeactivateAccount();

  // Build ProfileView from AuthContext profile (no extra fetch)
  const profileView = useMemo(() => {
    if (!profile) return null;
    return profileService.buildProfileView(profile);
  }, [profile]);

  // ─── Event Handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  const handleSignOutPress = useCallback(() => {
    Alert.alert(
      'Sign Out',
      'Are you sure you want to sign out?',
      [
        { text: 'Cancel', style: 'cancel' },
        {
          text:    'Sign Out',
          style:   'destructive',
          onPress: handleSignOut,
        },
      ]
    );
  }, [handleSignOut]);

  const handleDeactivatePress = useCallback(() => {
    Alert.alert(
      'Deactivate Account',
      'This will permanently deactivate your account. Your data will be retained for 30 days before deletion. This cannot be undone.',
      [
        { text: 'Cancel', style: 'cancel' },
        {
          text:    'Deactivate',
          style:   'destructive',
          onPress: () => {
            Alert.alert(
              'Final Confirmation',
              'Are you absolutely sure? Tap "Confirm" to deactivate.',
              [
                { text: 'Go Back',  style: 'cancel' },
                {
                  text:    'Confirm',
                  style:   'destructive',
                  onPress: () => deactivate(),
                },
              ]
            );
          },
        },
      ]
    );
  }, [deactivate]);

  const handleToggle = useCallback(
    (key: keyof ProfileSettings, value: boolean) => {
      updateSetting(key, value);
    },
    [updateSetting]
  );

  // ─── Settings Config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  const settingsSections = useMemo<SettingsSectionConfig[]>(() => [
    {
      title: 'Preferences',
      rows: [
        {
          id:               'notifications',
          label:            'Push Notifications',
          sublabel:         'Nearby bathroom alerts and code updates',
          icon:             '🔔',
          actionType:       'toggle',
          toggleKey:        'notifications_enabled',
          value:            settings.notifications_enabled,
          accessibilityHint: 'Toggle push notification alerts',
        },
        {
          id:               'accessibility_mode',
          label:            'Accessibility Mode',
          sublabel:         'Prioritize wheelchair-accessible bathrooms',
          icon:             '♿',
          actionType:       'navigate',
          route:            '/modal/accessibility-settings',
          onPress:          () => { /* Navigate to accessibility settings */ },
          accessibilityHint: 'Opens accessibility filter preferences',
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      title: 'About',
      rows: [
        {
          id:               'privacy',
          label:            'Privacy Policy',
          icon:             '🔒',
          actionType:       'external',
          onPress:          () => Linking.openURL('https://peedom.com/privacy'),
          accessibilityHint: 'Opens Privacy Policy in browser',
        },
        {
          id:               'terms',
          label:            'Terms of Service',
          icon:             '📄',
          actionType:       'external',
          onPress:          () => Linking.openURL('https://peedom.com/terms'),
          accessibilityHint: 'Opens Terms of Service in browser',
        },
        {
          id:               'version',
          label:            'App Version',
          sublabel:         '1.0.0 (build 1)',
          icon:             'ℹ️',
          actionType:       'navigate',
          accessibilityHint: 'Shows current application version',
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      title: 'Account',
      rows: [
        {
          id:               'signout',
          label:            'Sign Out',
          icon:             '🚪',
          actionType:       'destructive',
          onPress:          handleSignOutPress,
          destructiveLabel: 'Sign Out',
          accessibilityHint: 'Signs you out of your account',
        },
        {
          id:               'deactivate',
          label:            'Deactivate Account',
          icon:             '⚠️',
          actionType:       'destructive',
          onPress:          handleDeactivatePress,
          destructiveLabel: 'Deactivate',
          accessibilityHint: 'Permanently deactivates your account after confirmation',
        },
      ],
    },
  ], [settings, handleSignOutPress, handleDeactivatePress]);

  // ─── Render ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Guest state
  if (!isAuthenticated || !profileView) {
    return (
      <SafeAreaView style={styles.container} edges={['top', 'left', 'right']}>
        <GuestProfileView />
      </SafeAreaView>
    );
  }

  return (
    <SafeAreaView style={styles.container} edges={['top', 'left', 'right']}>
      <ScrollView
        style={styles.scroll}
        contentContainerStyle={styles.scrollContent}
        showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false}
      >
        {/* Header: Avatar + Name + Edit */}
        <ProfileHeader
          profile={profileView}
          onEditPress={() => setIsEditSheetOpen(true)}
        />

        {/* Stats Row */}
        <StatsRow stats={stats} isLoading={statsLoading} />

        {/* Points Badge */}
        <View style={styles.section}>
          <PointsBadge points={profileView.points_balance} />
        </View>

        {/* Premium Banner */}
        <PremiumBanner
          isPremium={profileView.is_premium}
          onUpgrade={() => Linking.openURL('https://peedom.com/premium')}
        />

        {/* Settings Sections */}
        {settingsSections.map((section) => (
          <SettingsSection
            key={section.title}
            section={section}
            onToggle={handleToggle}
          />
        ))}

        {/* Bottom padding for tab bar */}
        <View style={styles.bottomPadding} />
      </ScrollView>

      {/* Edit Display Name Sheet */}
      <EditDisplayNameSheet
        currentDisplayName={profileView.display_name}
        isOpen={isEditSheetOpen}
        onClose={() => setIsEditSheetOpen(false)}
      />
    </SafeAreaView>
  );
}

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  container: {
    flex:            1,
    backgroundColor: '#f9fafb',
  },
  scroll: {
    flex: 1,
  },
  scrollContent: {
    flexGrow: 1,
  },
  section: {
    paddingHorizontal: 16,
    paddingTop:        12,
  },
  bottomPadding: {
    height: 100,
  },
});
```

---

### Phase 13.9: Hardened AuthContext signOut

**File: `src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`** — `signOut` method replacement only

The existing `signOut` only clears `OFFLINE_QUEUE` and `RETURN_INTENT`. This is insufficient — it leaves TanStack Query cache populated with the previous user's data. The comprehensive purge is handled by `useSignOut()` in `useProfile.ts` which orchestrates the full teardown. The AuthContext `signOut` remains as the Supabase-layer sign-out primitive; all orchestration lives in the hook.

Replace only the `signOut` callback inside `AuthProvider`:

```typescript
// In src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx — replace the signOut useCallback

const signOut = useCallback(async () => {
  try {
    // Clear auth-dependent local storage
    await storage.remove(storage.keys.OFFLINE_QUEUE);
    await storage.remove(storage.keys.RETURN_INTENT);
    await storage.remove(storage.keys.CACHED_FAVORITES);
    // Supabase sign-out clears JWT + AsyncStorage auth tokens
    await supabase.auth.signOut();
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('AuthContext.signOut error:', error);
    // Never rethrow — local state cleared, JWT will expire naturally
  }
}, []);
```

---

### Phase 13.10: Validation Block

**File: `__tests__/services/ProfileService.test.ts`**

```typescript
import { profileService } from '@/services/ProfileService';
import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
import type { UserProfile } from '@/types/index';

jest.mock('@/lib/supabase', () => ({
  supabase: { rpc: jest.fn() },
}));

const mockProfile: UserProfile = {
  id:             'user-uuid-1',
  email:          'jane.doe@example.com',
  display_name:   'Jane Doe',
  role:           'user',
  points_balance: 247,
  is_premium:     false,
  is_suspended:   false,
  created_at:     '2025-01-15T08:00:00Z',
};

describe('ProfileService', () => {
  beforeEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks());

  describe('getContributionStats()', () => {
    it('maps BigInt RPC values to numbers', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: [{
          user_id:               'user-uuid-1',
          bathrooms_added:       BigInt(3),
          codes_submitted:       BigInt(12),
          votes_cast:            BigInt(47),
          reports_filed:         BigInt(2),
          ratings_submitted:     BigInt(8),
          lifetime_points_earned: BigInt(120),
        }],
        error: null,
      });

      const stats = await profileService.getContributionStats('user-uuid-1');

      expect(typeof stats.bathrooms_added).toBe('number');
      expect(stats.bathrooms_added).toBe(3);
      expect(stats.codes_submitted).toBe(12);
    });

    it('returns empty stats for new user with no contributions', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: [], error: null,
      });

      const stats = await profileService.getContributionStats('user-uuid-new');

      expect(stats.bathrooms_added).toBe(0);
      expect(stats.lifetime_points_earned).toBe(0);
    });

    it('throws AUTH_REQUIRED when server rejects unauthenticated call', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data:  null,
        error: { message: 'AUTH_REQUIRED: Cannot fetch contribution stats' },
      });

      await expect(
        profileService.getContributionStats('user-uuid-1')
      ).rejects.toThrow('AUTH_REQUIRED');
    });
  });

  describe('updateDisplayName()', () => {
    it('rejects display name shorter than 2 chars (client-side Zod)', async () => {
      await expect(
        profileService.updateDisplayName('A')
      ).rejects.toThrow();
      expect(supabase.rpc).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    it('rejects display name with only whitespace', async () => {
      await expect(
        profileService.updateDisplayName('   ')
      ).rejects.toThrow();
      expect(supabase.rpc).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    it('trims whitespace before submitting', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data: { success: true, user_id: 'user-uuid-1', updated_at: new Date().toISOString() },
        error: null,
      });

      await profileService.updateDisplayName('  Jane Doe  ');

      expect(supabase.rpc).toHaveBeenCalledWith('update_profile', {
        p_display_name: 'Jane Doe',   // Trimmed
      });
    });

    it('surfaces RATE_LIMITED error with user-friendly message', async () => {
      (supabase.rpc as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
        data:  null,
        error: { message: 'RATE_LIMITED: Display name can only be changed once every 24 hours' },
      });

      await expect(
        profileService.updateDisplayName('New Name')
      ).rejects.toThrow('RATE_LIMITED');
    });
  });

  describe('buildProfileView()', () => {
    it('computes correct initials for two-word display name', () => {
      const view = profileService.buildProfileView(mockProfile);
      expect(view.initials).toBe('JD');
    });

    it('computes correct initials for single-word display name', () => {
      const view = profileService.buildProfileView({
        ...mockProfile, display_name: 'Madonna',
      });
      expect(view.initials).toBe('MA');
    });

    it('falls back to email initials when display_name is null', () => {
      const view = profileService.buildProfileView({
        ...mockProfile, display_name: null,
      });
      expect(view.initials).toBe('JA'); // "ja" from "jane..."
    });

    it('maps role to human-readable label', () => {
      const view = profileService.buildProfileView(mockProfile);
      expect(view.role_label).toBe('Community Member');
    });

    it('formats member_since_label from created_at', () => {
      const view = profileService.buildProfileView(mockProfile);
      expect(view.member_since_label).toContain('January 2025');
    });

    it('correctly identifies Silver tier for 247 points', () => {
      // PointsBadge tier logic is separate — tested there
      // ProfileView exposes raw points_balance for PointsBadge to consume
      const view = profileService.buildProfileView(mockProfile);
      expect(view.points_balance).toBe(247);
    });
  });
});
```

**File: `__tests__/validators/profile.test.ts`**

```typescript
import {
  validateUpdateProfile,
  validateDeactivationConfirm,
} from '@/validators/profile';

describe('validateUpdateProfile()', () => {
  it('passes valid display name', () => {
    expect(() => validateUpdateProfile({ display_name: 'Jane Doe' })).not.toThrow();
  });

  it('trims whitespace before validation', () => {
    const result = validateUpdateProfile({ display_name: '  Jane  ' });
    expect(result.display_name).toBe('Jane');
  });

  it('rejects single character after trimming', () => {
    expect(() => validateUpdateProfile({ display_name: '  A  ' })).toThrow();
  });

  it('rejects name exceeding 50 characters', () => {
    expect(() =>
      validateUpdateProfile({ display_name: 'A'.repeat(51) })
    ).toThrow();
  });

  it('accepts exactly 50 characters', () => {
    expect(() =>
      validateUpdateProfile({ display_name: 'A'.repeat(50) })
    ).not.toThrow();
  });
});

describe('validateDeactivationConfirm()', () => {
  it('returns true for "DELETE"',         () => expect(validateDeactivationConfirm('DELETE')).toBe(true));
  it('returns true for "delete"',         () => expect(validateDeactivationConfirm('delete')).toBe(true));
  it('returns true for "  DELETE  "',     () => expect(validateDeactivationConfirm('  DELETE  ')).toBe(true));
  it('returns false for "DELET"',         () => expect(validateDeactivationConfirm('DELET')).toBe(false));
  it('returns false for empty string',    () => expect(validateDeactivationConfirm('')).toBe(false));
  it('returns false for "delete account"', () => expect(validateDeactivationConfirm('delete account')).toBe(false));
});
```

```
                                            destructive/external)

                            declarative settings config, two-step deactivation Alert
```

**Integration checklist:**
```
□ Run migration: supabase db push (007_profile_infrastructure.sql)
□ Verify view: SELECT * FROM v_user_contribution_stats WHERE user_id = '<your-test-user-id>';
□ npm run type-check — strict mode must pass
□ npm test -- ProfileService profile.test
□ Smoke: Guest → Profile tab → GuestProfileView renders with sign-in button
□ Smoke: Authenticated → Profile tab → header shows correct initials + name
□ Smoke: Tap edit (✏️) → EditDisplayNameSheet opens → type "A" → Save disabled
□ Smoke: Type "Test Name" → Save enabled → tap Save → header updates
□ Smoke: Type name twice in 24h → RATE_LIMITED error shows inline
□ Smoke: Sign Out → Alert → confirm → all tabs reset to unauthenticated state
□ Smoke: Profile tab stats show "—" while loading → values appear after fetch
```

**Performance notes:**
- `buildProfileView()` is a pure synchronous function — zero async overhead on render
- `v_user_contribution_stats` aggregates across 5 tables with FK indexes; p99 target <200ms
- `getAvatarColor()` is a deterministic hash — O(N) where N = userId length (~36 chars); negligible
- `StatsRow` uses `memo()` with default comparator — safe because `stats` object reference changes only on TanStack Query refetch
- Settings sections built with `useMemo([settings, ...handlers])` — stable between renders unless settings or handlers change

## Pre-Flight Simulation — Sections 14 & 15

**Scanning full dependency graph across both sections:**

**Section 14 verified foundations:**
- `v_bathroom_detail_public` view exists but lacks viewer state (`is_favorited`, `user_vote`, `can_submit_code`) — anonymous RPC projection needed ✓
- `useRealtimeCodeVotes` hook from Section 10 ready to wire ✓
- `useToggleFavorite` from Section 12 ready to wire ✓
- `useRealtimePresence` from Section 10 ready to wire ✓
- `BathroomDetail` + `ViewerState` types exist in `src/types/index.ts` ✓
- `@gorhom/bottom-sheet` in package.json ✓

**Section 15 verified foundations:**
- `add_bathroom`, `submit_code`, `vote_code` RPCs — none exist ✗

- `AddBathroomDraft` / `ClaimBusinessDraft` types defined ✓

- `returnIntent` pattern wired for guest-intent replay ✓

**Critical architectural decisions locked before writing a line:**

Section 14 — `get_bathroom_detail` must be a single RPC returning a merged projection of bathroom + code + viewer state. Fetching these separately creates 3 round trips and a viewer-state race condition (user votes while detail is loading). One atomic call prevents this.

Section 15 — `add_bathroom` and `submit_code` must be SECURITY DEFINER with explicit ownership checks. Direct RLS inserts from the client bypass business logic (e.g., duplicate bathroom detection, code deduplication for the same bathroom).

**Dependency graph:**

```
Section 14 creates:
- supabase/migrations/008_bathroom_detail.sql
- src/types/bathroom.ts
- src/validators/bathroom.ts
- src/services/BathroomService.ts
- src/hooks/useBathroomDetail.ts
- src/hooks/useCodeVote.ts
- src/components/detail/BathroomDetailHeader.tsx
- src/components/detail/CodeCard.tsx
- src/components/detail/VoteButtons.tsx
- src/components/detail/CleanlinessRating.tsx
- src/components/detail/PresenceIndicator.tsx
- src/components/detail/ActionSheet.tsx
- app/bathroom/[id].tsx  (full replacement)

Section 15 creates:
- supabase/migrations/009_contribution_mutations.sql

- src/hooks/useContributions.ts

- app/modal/report.tsx       (full replacement)
```

## Section 14: Bathroom Detail Screen

### Phase 14.1: Database — Enriched Detail Projection

**File: `supabase/migrations/008_bathroom_detail.sql`**

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- Bathroom Detail Infrastructure
-- Single-RPC enriched projection with viewer state + code list + metrics
--
-- ARCHITECTURE:
-- The detail screen requires data from 6 tables:
--   bathrooms, bathroom_access_codes, code_votes, favorites,
--   cleanliness_ratings, bathroom_reports
--
-- Fetching these with sequential client queries creates:
-- 1. 6x network round trips (200ms each = 1.2s total)
-- 2. Viewer state race condition (user state read between bathroom fetch and code fetch)
-- 3. Inconsistent snapshot (bathroom updated between requests)
--
-- Solution: Two RPCs
--   get_bathroom_detail    → main projection + viewer state + metrics
--   get_bathroom_codes     → paginated code list for this bathroom
--
-- Separation rationale: Codes can have many rows (50+). Embedding them in
-- the detail RPC inflates payload size for every detail view. Pagination
-- is cleaner as a separate call.
-- ============================================================================

-- ============================================================================
-- GET BATHROOM DETAIL (main projection)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_bathroom_detail(
  p_bathroom_id UUID,
  p_user_id     UUID DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_result       JSONB;
  v_bathroom     RECORD;
  v_code         RECORD;
  v_viewer       RECORD;
  v_metrics      RECORD;
BEGIN
  -- Fetch bathroom (active only)
  SELECT
    b.id, b.place_name, b.address_line1, b.city, b.state,
    b.postal_code, b.country_code, b.latitude, b.longitude,
    b.is_locked, b.is_accessible, b.is_customer_only,
    b.hours_json, b.source_type, b.updated_at
  INTO v_bathroom
  FROM public.bathrooms b
  WHERE b.id = p_bathroom_id AND b.moderation_status = 'active';

  IF NOT FOUND THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'NOT_FOUND: Bathroom does not exist or has been removed';
  END IF;

  -- Fetch best available code (highest confidence, most recent)
  SELECT
    c.id, c.code_value, c.confidence_score,
    c.up_votes, c.down_votes, c.last_verified_at, c.expires_at
  INTO v_code
  FROM public.bathroom_access_codes c
  WHERE c.bathroom_id = p_bathroom_id
    AND c.visibility_status = 'visible'
    AND c.lifecycle_status  = 'active'
  ORDER BY c.confidence_score DESC, c.last_verified_at DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 1;

  -- Fetch community metrics
  SELECT
    AVG(r.rating)::NUMERIC(3,2)         AS avg_cleanliness,
    COUNT(DISTINCT r.id)                AS rating_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT rpt.id)
      FILTER (WHERE rpt.status = 'open') AS open_report_count
  INTO v_metrics
  FROM public.bathrooms b
  LEFT JOIN public.cleanliness_ratings r
    ON r.bathroom_id = b.id
  LEFT JOIN public.bathroom_reports rpt
    ON rpt.bathroom_id = b.id
  WHERE b.id = p_bathroom_id
  GROUP BY b.id;

  -- Fetch viewer state (only meaningful for authenticated users)
  IF p_user_id IS NOT NULL THEN
    SELECT
      EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.favorites f
        WHERE f.user_id = p_user_id AND f.bathroom_id = p_bathroom_id
      ) AS is_favorited,

      -- User's vote on the primary code (NULL if no code or no vote)
      (
        SELECT cv.vote FROM public.code_votes cv
        WHERE cv.user_id = p_user_id AND cv.code_id = v_code.id
      ) AS user_vote_on_primary_code,

      -- Can this user submit a code?
      -- Rule: Must not have submitted a code for this bathroom in the last 7 days
      NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.bathroom_access_codes c
        WHERE c.submitted_by = p_user_id
          AND c.bathroom_id  = p_bathroom_id
          AND c.created_at   > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
      ) AS can_submit_code,

      -- Has this user already rated cleanliness?
      EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.cleanliness_ratings cr
        WHERE cr.user_id = p_user_id AND cr.bathroom_id = p_bathroom_id
      ) AS has_rated_cleanliness

    INTO v_viewer;
  END IF;

  -- Build result JSON
  v_result := jsonb_build_object(
    'id',            v_bathroom.id,
    'place_name',    v_bathroom.place_name,
    'address', jsonb_build_object(
      'line1',        v_bathroom.address_line1,
      'city',         v_bathroom.city,
      'state',        v_bathroom.state,
      'postal_code',  v_bathroom.postal_code,
      'country_code', v_bathroom.country_code
    ),
    'coordinates', jsonb_build_object(
      'latitude',  v_bathroom.latitude,
      'longitude', v_bathroom.longitude
    ),
    'flags', jsonb_build_object(
      'is_locked',        v_bathroom.is_locked,
      'is_accessible',    v_bathroom.is_accessible,
      'is_customer_only', v_bathroom.is_customer_only
    ),
    'hours',      v_bathroom.hours_json,
    'source_type', v_bathroom.source_type,
    'updated_at',  v_bathroom.updated_at,

    -- Primary code (null if none)
    'primary_code', CASE
      WHEN v_code.id IS NOT NULL THEN jsonb_build_object(
        'id',               v_code.id,
        'code_value',       v_code.code_value,
        'confidence_score', v_code.confidence_score,
        'up_votes',         v_code.up_votes,
        'down_votes',       v_code.down_votes,
        'last_verified_at', v_code.last_verified_at,
        'expires_at',       v_code.expires_at
      )
      ELSE NULL
    END,

    -- Community metrics
    'community', jsonb_build_object(
      'avg_cleanliness',  COALESCE(v_metrics.avg_cleanliness, 0),
      'rating_count',     COALESCE(v_metrics.rating_count, 0),
      'open_report_count', COALESCE(v_metrics.open_report_count, 0)
    ),

    -- Viewer state (null for guests)
    'viewer_state', CASE
      WHEN p_user_id IS NOT NULL THEN jsonb_build_object(
        'is_favorited',            COALESCE(v_viewer.is_favorited, false),
        'user_vote_on_primary_code', v_viewer.user_vote_on_primary_code,
        'can_submit_code',         COALESCE(v_viewer.can_submit_code, true),
        'has_rated_cleanliness',   COALESCE(v_viewer.has_rated_cleanliness, false)
      )
      ELSE jsonb_build_object(
        'is_favorited',            false,
        'user_vote_on_primary_code', NULL,
        'can_submit_code',         true,
        'has_rated_cleanliness',   false
      )
    END
  );

  RETURN v_result;
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- GET BATHROOM CODES (paginated code list)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_bathroom_codes(
  p_bathroom_id UUID,
  p_user_id     UUID DEFAULT NULL,
  p_limit       INTEGER DEFAULT 10,
  p_offset      INTEGER DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id                UUID,
  code_value        TEXT,
  confidence_score  NUMERIC,
  up_votes          INTEGER,
  down_votes        INTEGER,
  last_verified_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
  expires_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  submitted_by_name TEXT,   -- Display name (or 'Anonymous')
  user_vote         SMALLINT  -- Caller's vote (-1, 1, or NULL)
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    c.id,
    c.code_value,
    c.confidence_score,
    c.up_votes,
    c.down_votes,
    c.last_verified_at,
    c.expires_at,
    COALESCE(p.display_name, 'Community Member') AS submitted_by_name,
    (
      SELECT cv.vote FROM public.code_votes cv
      WHERE cv.user_id = p_user_id AND cv.code_id = c.id
    ) AS user_vote
  FROM public.bathroom_access_codes c
  LEFT JOIN public.profiles p ON p.id = c.submitted_by
  WHERE c.bathroom_id      = p_bathroom_id
    AND c.visibility_status = 'visible'
    AND c.lifecycle_status  = 'active'
  ORDER BY c.confidence_score DESC, c.last_verified_at DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT  LEAST(p_limit, 20)
  OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- VOTE ON CODE (atomic upsert)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.vote_on_code(
  p_code_id UUID,
  p_vote    SMALLINT  -- 1 = upvote, -1 = downvote, 0 = retract vote
)
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_user_id   UUID;
  v_bathroom_id UUID;
  v_existing  RECORD;
  v_action    TEXT;
BEGIN
  v_user_id := auth.uid();
  IF v_user_id IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'AUTH_REQUIRED: Must be authenticated to vote';
  END IF;

  IF p_vote NOT IN (-1, 0, 1) THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'VALIDATION_ERROR: Vote must be -1, 0, or 1';
  END IF;

  -- Verify code exists
  SELECT c.bathroom_id INTO v_bathroom_id
  FROM public.bathroom_access_codes c
  WHERE c.id = p_code_id
    AND c.visibility_status = 'visible'
    AND c.lifecycle_status  = 'active';

  IF NOT FOUND THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'NOT_FOUND: Code does not exist or has been removed';
  END IF;

  -- Prevent self-voting
  IF EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.bathroom_access_codes c
    WHERE c.id = p_code_id AND c.submitted_by = v_user_id
  ) THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'FORBIDDEN: Cannot vote on your own submitted code';
  END IF;

  -- Check existing vote
  SELECT id, vote INTO v_existing
  FROM public.code_votes
  WHERE code_id = p_code_id AND user_id = v_user_id;

  IF p_vote = 0 THEN
    -- Retract vote
    IF v_existing.id IS NOT NULL THEN
      DELETE FROM public.code_votes WHERE id = v_existing.id;
      v_action := 'retracted';
    ELSE
      v_action := 'no_change';
    END IF;
  ELSIF v_existing.id IS NOT NULL THEN
    IF v_existing.vote = p_vote THEN
      -- Same vote → retract (toggle behavior)
      DELETE FROM public.code_votes WHERE id = v_existing.id;
      v_action := 'retracted';
    ELSE
      -- Different vote → update
      UPDATE public.code_votes
      SET vote = p_vote, updated_at = NOW()
      WHERE id = v_existing.id;
      v_action := 'changed';
    END IF;
  ELSE
    -- New vote
    INSERT INTO public.code_votes (code_id, user_id, vote)
    VALUES (p_code_id, v_user_id, p_vote);
    v_action := 'cast';
  END IF;

  RETURN jsonb_build_object(
    'action',      v_action,
    'code_id',     p_code_id,
    'vote',        p_vote,
    'voted_at',    NOW()
  );
END;
$$;

-- ============================================================================
-- SUBMIT CLEANLINESS RATING (upsert — one rating per user per bathroom)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.rate_cleanliness(
  p_bathroom_id UUID,
  p_rating      SMALLINT,
  p_notes       TEXT DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_user_id UUID;
BEGIN
  v_user_id := auth.uid();
  IF v_user_id IS NULL THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'AUTH_REQUIRED: Must be authenticated to rate';
  END IF;

  IF p_rating < 1 OR p_rating > 5 THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'VALIDATION_ERROR: Rating must be between 1 and 5';
  END IF;

  INSERT INTO public.cleanliness_ratings (bathroom_id, user_id, rating, notes)
  VALUES (p_bathroom_id, v_user_id, p_rating, p_notes)
  ON CONFLICT (bathroom_id, user_id) DO UPDATE
    SET rating = EXCLUDED.rating,
        notes  = EXCLUDED.notes;

  RETURN jsonb_build_object(
    'success',      true,
    'bathroom_id',  p_bathroom_id,
    'rating',       p_rating,
    'rated_at',     NOW()
  );
END;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.get_bathroom_detail(UUID, UUID)               TO anon, authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.get_bathroom_codes(UUID, UUID, INTEGER, INTEGER) TO anon, authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.vote_on_code(UUID, SMALLINT)                  TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.rate_cleanliness(UUID, SMALLINT, TEXT)        TO authenticated;
```

---

### Phase 14.2: TypeScript Bathroom Detail Types

**File: `src/types/bathroom.ts`**

```typescript
/**
 * Bathroom Detail Domain Types
 *
 * ARCHITECTURAL DECISION:
 * We define a separate `BathroomDetailView` type that is the canonical
 * shape returned by get_bathroom_detail() RPC, post-transformation.
 * The existing `BathroomDetail` in src/types/index.ts is retained for
 * backwards compatibility but BathroomDetailView is the authoritative
 * type for the detail screen and all components consuming it.
 *
 * CODE VISIBILITY TIERS:
 * 'shown'         → user can see the code value (authenticated OR free tier)
 * 'premium_gated' → code exists but user needs premium to view the value
 * 'hidden'        → no code exists for this bathroom
 *
 * MVP: All authenticated users see codes. Premium gating is a Phase 2 feature.
 * The type models the full future state today to prevent breaking changes later.
 */

// ============================================================================
// DETAIL SCREEN VIEW TYPES
// ============================================================================

export interface BathroomAddress {
  line1:        string | null;
  city:         string | null;
  state:        string | null;
  postal_code:  string | null;
  country_code: string;
  formatted:    string;   // Pre-built: "85 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101"
}

export interface BathroomCoordinates {
  latitude:  number;
  longitude: number;
}

export interface BathroomFlags {
  is_locked:        boolean | null;
  is_accessible:    boolean | null;
  is_customer_only: boolean;
}

export interface PrimaryCodeView {
  id:               string | null;
  visibility:       'shown' | 'premium_gated' | 'hidden';
  code_value:       string | null;
  confidence_score: number | null;
  confidence_label: string;    // "87% confident" | "Unverified"
  up_votes:         number;
  down_votes:       number;
  last_verified_at: string | null;
  last_verified_label: string | null;  // "Verified 2 days ago"
  expires_at:       string | null;
  is_expired:       boolean;
}

export interface CommunityMetricsView {
  avg_cleanliness:    number;
  rating_count:       number;
  open_report_count:  number;
  cleanliness_label:  string;   // "4.2 ★" | "No ratings yet"
}

export interface ViewerStateView {
  is_favorited:              boolean;
  user_vote_on_primary_code: -1 | 1 | null;
  can_submit_code:           boolean;
  has_rated_cleanliness:     boolean;
}

export interface BathroomDetailView {
  id:           string;
  place_name:   string;
  address:      BathroomAddress;
  coordinates:  BathroomCoordinates;
  flags:        BathroomFlags;
  hours:        Record<string, Array<{ open: string; close: string }>> | null;
  source_type:  'community' | 'business' | 'imported' | 'admin';
  updated_at:   string;
  primary_code: PrimaryCodeView;
  community:    CommunityMetricsView;
  viewer_state: ViewerStateView;
}

// ============================================================================
// CODE LIST TYPES
// ============================================================================

export interface CodeListItem {
  id:               string;
  code_value:       string;
  confidence_score: number;
  confidence_label: string;
  up_votes:         number;
  down_votes:       number;
  last_verified_at: string | null;
  last_verified_label: string | null;
  expires_at:       string | null;
  is_expired:       boolean;
  submitted_by_name: string;
  user_vote:        -1 | 1 | null;
}

// ============================================================================
// VOTE TYPES
// ============================================================================

export type VoteValue = -1 | 0 | 1;

export interface VoteOutcome {
  action:    'cast' | 'changed' | 'retracted' | 'no_change';
  code_id:   string;
  vote:      VoteValue;
  voted_at:  string;
}

// ============================================================================
// CLEANLINESS RATING
// ============================================================================

export interface CleanlinessRatingInput {
  bathroom_id: string;
  rating:      1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5;
  notes?:      string;
}
```

---

### Phase 14.3: Bathroom Service

**File: `src/services/BathroomService.ts`**

```typescript
import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
import type {
  BathroomDetailView,
  CodeListItem,
  VoteOutcome,
  VoteValue,
  PrimaryCodeView,
  CommunityMetricsView,
  ViewerStateView,
  BathroomAddress,
} from '@/types/bathroom';

/**
 * BathroomService
 *
 * TRANSFORMATION STRATEGY:
 * get_bathroom_detail returns a single JSONB object (not a typed row).
 * We cast it through a chain of pure transformer functions. Each transformer
 * handles one sub-object (address, code, metrics, viewer state).
 * This makes each transformation unit-testable in isolation.
 *
 * RELATIVE TIME LABELS:
 * "Verified 2 days ago" and "Member since..." are computed at transform time,
 * not at render time. This prevents a subtle bug where the label would update
 * mid-render if recomputed inside a component on every re-render.
 * The correct approach: compute once at service boundary, stable in cache.
 * Trade-off: A "2 days ago" label becomes "3 days ago" only after a refetch.
 * This is acceptable — detail screens refetch on focus (staleTime = 2 minutes).
 */

class BathroomService {
  async getBathroomDetail(
    bathroomId: string,
    userId?: string | null
  ): Promise<BathroomDetailView> {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('get_bathroom_detail', {
      p_bathroom_id: bathroomId,
      p_user_id:     userId ?? null,
    });

    if (error) {
      if (error.message.includes('NOT_FOUND')) {
        throw new Error('NOT_FOUND: Bathroom does not exist or has been removed');
      }
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    return this.transformDetailResponse(data as Record<string, unknown>);
  }

  async getBathroomCodes(
    bathroomId: string,
    userId?: string | null,
    offset = 0
  ): Promise<CodeListItem[]> {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('get_bathroom_codes', {
      p_bathroom_id: bathroomId,
      p_user_id:     userId ?? null,
      p_limit:       10,
      p_offset:      offset,
    });

    if (error) {
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    return ((data ?? []) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>).map((row) =>
      this.transformCodeRow(row)
    );
  }

  async voteOnCode(
    codeId: string,
    vote:   VoteValue
  ): Promise<VoteOutcome> {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('vote_on_code', {
      p_code_id: codeId,
      p_vote:    vote,
    });

    if (error) {
      if (error.message.includes('AUTH_REQUIRED'))  throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED');
      if (error.message.includes('NOT_FOUND'))      throw new Error('NOT_FOUND: Code removed');
      if (error.message.includes('FORBIDDEN'))      throw new Error('FORBIDDEN: Cannot vote on own code');
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }

    return data as VoteOutcome;
  }

  async rateCleanliness(
    bathroomId: string,
    rating: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5,
    notes?: string
  ): Promise<void> {
    const { error } = await supabase.rpc('rate_cleanliness', {
      p_bathroom_id: bathroomId,
      p_rating:      rating,
      p_notes:       notes ?? null,
    });

    if (error) {
      if (error.message.includes('AUTH_REQUIRED')) throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED');
      throw new Error(`NETWORK_ERROR: ${error.message}`);
    }
  }

  // ── Private Transformers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  private transformDetailResponse(
    raw: Record<string, unknown>
  ): BathroomDetailView {
    const addr   = raw.address   as Record<string, unknown>;
    const coords = raw.coordinates as Record<string, unknown>;
    const flags  = raw.flags     as Record<string, unknown>;
    const code   = raw.primary_code as Record<string, unknown> | null;
    const comm   = raw.community as Record<string, unknown>;
    const viewer = raw.viewer_state as Record<string, unknown>;

    return {
      id:          raw.id as string,
      place_name:  raw.place_name as string,
      address:     this.buildAddress(addr),
      coordinates: {
        latitude:  coords.latitude as number,
        longitude: coords.longitude as number,
      },
      flags: {
        is_locked:        (flags.is_locked as boolean) ?? null,
        is_accessible:    (flags.is_accessible as boolean) ?? null,
        is_customer_only: (flags.is_customer_only as boolean) ?? false,
      },
      hours:       (raw.hours as Record<string, unknown> | null) as any,
      source_type: raw.source_type as BathroomDetailView['source_type'],
      updated_at:  raw.updated_at as string,
      primary_code: this.buildPrimaryCode(code),
      community:    this.buildMetrics(comm),
      viewer_state: this.buildViewerState(viewer),
    };
  }

  private buildAddress(addr: Record<string, unknown>): BathroomAddress {
    const parts: string[] = [];
    if (addr.line1) parts.push(addr.line1 as string);
    const cityState: string[] = [];
    if (addr.city)  cityState.push(addr.city  as string);
    if (addr.state) cityState.push(addr.state as string);
    if (cityState.length) parts.push(cityState.join(', '));
    if (addr.postal_code) parts.push(addr.postal_code as string);

    return {
      line1:        (addr.line1 as string) ?? null,
      city:         (addr.city  as string) ?? null,
      state:        (addr.state as string) ?? null,
      postal_code:  (addr.postal_code as string) ?? null,
      country_code: (addr.country_code as string) ?? 'US',
      formatted:    parts.join(', ') || 'Address unavailable',
    };
  }

  private buildPrimaryCode(
    code: Record<string, unknown> | null
  ): PrimaryCodeView {
    if (!code) {
      return {
        id: null, visibility: 'hidden', code_value: null,
        confidence_score: null, confidence_label: 'No code available',
        up_votes: 0, down_votes: 0,
        last_verified_at: null, last_verified_label: null,
        expires_at: null, is_expired: false,
      };
    }

    const confidence = code.confidence_score as number | null;
    const expiresAt  = code.expires_at as string | null;
    const isExpired  = expiresAt ? new Date(expiresAt) < new Date() : false;
    const lastVerifiedAt = code.last_verified_at as string | null;

    return {
      id:               code.id as string,
      visibility:       'shown',
      code_value:       code.code_value as string,
      confidence_score: confidence,
      confidence_label: confidence != null
        ? `${Math.round(confidence)}% confident`
        : 'Unverified',
      up_votes:         (code.up_votes   as number) ?? 0,
      down_votes:       (code.down_votes as number) ?? 0,
      last_verified_at: lastVerifiedAt,
      last_verified_label: lastVerifiedAt
        ? this.formatRelativeTime(lastVerifiedAt)
        : null,
      expires_at:  expiresAt,
      is_expired:  isExpired,
    };
  }

  private buildMetrics(comm: Record<string, unknown>): CommunityMetricsView {
    const avg   = (comm.avg_cleanliness  as number) ?? 0;
    const count = (comm.rating_count     as number) ?? 0;

    return {
      avg_cleanliness:   avg,
      rating_count:      count,
      open_report_count: (comm.open_report_count as number) ?? 0,
      cleanliness_label: count > 0
        ? `${avg.toFixed(1)} ★ (${count})`
        : 'No ratings yet',
    };
  }

  private buildViewerState(
    viewer: Record<string, unknown>
  ): ViewerStateView {
    return {
      is_favorited:              (viewer.is_favorited            as boolean) ?? false,
      user_vote_on_primary_code: (viewer.user_vote_on_primary_code as -1 | 1 | null) ?? null,
      can_submit_code:           (viewer.can_submit_code          as boolean) ?? true,
      has_rated_cleanliness:     (viewer.has_rated_cleanliness    as boolean) ?? false,
    };
  }

  private transformCodeRow(row: Record<string, unknown>): CodeListItem {
    const confidence    = row.confidence_score as number | null;
    const lastVerified  = row.last_verified_at as string | null;
    const expiresAt     = row.expires_at as string | null;

    return {
      id:               row.id as string,
      code_value:       row.code_value as string,
      confidence_score: confidence ?? 0,
      confidence_label: confidence != null
        ? `${Math.round(confidence)}%`
        : 'Unverified',
      up_votes:         (row.up_votes   as number) ?? 0,
      down_votes:       (row.down_votes as number) ?? 0,
      last_verified_at: lastVerified,
      last_verified_label: lastVerified
        ? this.formatRelativeTime(lastVerified)
        : null,
      expires_at:       expiresAt,
      is_expired:       expiresAt ? new Date(expiresAt) < new Date() : false,
      submitted_by_name: (row.submitted_by_name as string) ?? 'Community Member',
      user_vote:        (row.user_vote as -1 | 1 | null) ?? null,
    };
  }

  private formatRelativeTime(isoString: string): string {
    const diffMs    = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();
    const diffMins  = Math.floor(diffMs / 60000);
    if (diffMins < 1)    return 'just now';
    if (diffMins < 60)   return `${diffMins}m ago`;
    const diffHours = Math.floor(diffMins / 60);
    if (diffHours < 24)  return `${diffHours}h ago`;
    const diffDays  = Math.floor(diffHours / 24);
    if (diffDays < 7)    return `${diffDays}d ago`;
    return `${Math.floor(diffDays / 7)}w ago`;
  }
}

export const bathroomService = new BathroomService();
```

### Phase 14.4: TanStack Query Hooks

**File: `src/hooks/useBathroomDetail.ts`**

```typescript
import {
  useQuery,
  useMutation,
  useQueryClient,
  keepPreviousData,
} from '@tanstack/react-query';
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { bathroomService } from '@/services/BathroomService';
import { useFavoritesStore } from '@/stores/favoritesStore';
import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
import { returnIntent } from '@/lib/return-intent';
import type { BathroomDetailView, VoteValue } from '@/types/bathroom';

/**
 * Bathroom Detail Query Key Factory
 *
 * INVALIDATION HIERARCHY:
 * ['bathroom-detail'] → all detail screens (bulk invalidation on moderation)
 * ['bathroom-detail', id] → single bathroom (realtime or post-mutation)
 * ['bathroom-detail', id, 'codes'] → code list for this bathroom
 */
export const bathroomDetailKeys = {
  all:    ['bathroom-detail'] as const,
  detail: (id: string, userId?: string | null) =>
    [...bathroomDetailKeys.all, id, userId ?? 'anon'] as const,
  codes:  (id: string, userId?: string | null) =>
    [...bathroomDetailKeys.all, id, 'codes', userId ?? 'anon'] as const,
};

/**
 * useBathroomDetail
 *
 * STALE TIME: 2 minutes.
 * Detail data changes via: realtime code vote updates (Section 10),
 * explicit mutations (vote, rate, favorite). All mutations invalidate
 * this query key, so 2 minutes is the maximum possible staleness.
 *
 * REFETCH ON FOCUS:
 * True (default). When user navigates back to this screen from a modal
 * (e.g., after submitting a code), the detail refreshes immediately.
 * This is the correct behavior — the user expects to see their submission.
 *
 * VIEWER STATE DEPENDENCY:
 * The query key includes userId. When the user signs in mid-session
 * (after tapping favorite → auth required → sign in → returns),
 * the key changes and a fresh fetch retrieves the authenticated viewer state.
 * Without userId in the key, the guest-state response would be cached
 * and served to the now-authenticated user. Critical bug prevention.
 */
export function useBathroomDetail(bathroomId: string) {
  const { user } = useAuth();

  return useQuery({
    queryKey: bathroomDetailKeys.detail(bathroomId, user?.id),
    queryFn:  () =>
      bathroomService.getBathroomDetail(bathroomId, user?.id),
    staleTime:        2 * 60 * 1000,
    placeholderData:  keepPreviousData,
    retry: (failureCount, error) => {
      if (error instanceof Error && error.message.startsWith('NOT_FOUND')) {
        return false; // Non-retryable: bathroom removed
      }
      return failureCount < 2;
    },
  });
}

/**
 * useBathroomCodes
 *
 * Separate query for the codes list (below the primary code card).
 * Paginated — first 10 codes shown, user taps "Show all" for more.
 */
export function useBathroomCodes(bathroomId: string) {
  const { user } = useAuth();

  return useQuery({
    queryKey: bathroomDetailKeys.codes(bathroomId, user?.id),
    queryFn:  () =>
      bathroomService.getBathroomCodes(bathroomId, user?.id),
    staleTime: 2 * 60 * 1000,
    enabled:   !!bathroomId,
  });
}

/**
 * useVoteOnCode
 *
 * OPTIMISTIC UPDATE PATTERN:
 * 1. onMutate: Update the vote count in BathroomDetailView cache immediately.
 *    Increment up_votes or down_votes and set user_vote_on_primary_code.
 * 2. onError: Rollback via previousData snapshot.
 * 3. onSuccess: Invalidate codes list (other codes' vote counts may shift rank).
 *
 * TOGGLE BEHAVIOR:
 * If user votes 1 and taps upvote again → send vote=0 (retract).
 * The hook determines the correct vote value before calling the mutation:
 *   currentVote === incoming → retract (0)
 *   otherwise → set to incoming
 * This logic lives here (not in the UI) so it's consistent across
 * all surfaces that show vote buttons.
 *
 * AUTH GATE:
 * returnIntent set before pushing to login. On return, the intent
 * contains the bathroom_id and code_id to replay the vote.
 */
export function useVoteOnCode(bathroomId: string) {
  const queryClient        = useQueryClient();
  const { user, isAuthenticated } = useAuth();
  const isFavorited        = useFavoritesStore((s) => s.isFavorited);

  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: async ({
      codeId,
      incomingVote,
      currentVote,
    }: {
      codeId:       string;
      incomingVote: 1 | -1;
      currentVote:  -1 | 1 | null;
    }) => {
      if (!isAuthenticated || !user?.id) {
        await returnIntent.set({
          type:            'vote_code',
          route:           `/bathroom/${bathroomId}`,
          params:          { bathroom_id: bathroomId, code_id: codeId, vote: incomingVote },
          replay_strategy: 'immediate_after_auth',
        });
        throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED');
      }

      // Toggle: same vote = retract
      const effectiveVote: VoteValue =
        currentVote === incomingVote ? 0 : incomingVote;

      return bathroomService.voteOnCode(codeId, effectiveVote);
    },

    onMutate: async ({ codeId, incomingVote, currentVote }) => {
      const key = bathroomDetailKeys.detail(bathroomId, user?.id);
      await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: key });
      const previousData = queryClient.getQueryData<BathroomDetailView>(key);

      const effectiveVote: VoteValue =
        currentVote === incomingVote ? 0 : incomingVote;

      // Optimistically update vote counts on primary code
      queryClient.setQueryData<BathroomDetailView>(key, (old) => {
        if (!old || old.primary_code.id !== codeId) return old;

        const delta = (v: number, sign: number) =>
          Math.max(0, v + sign);

        let upVotes   = old.primary_code.up_votes;
        let downVotes = old.primary_code.down_votes;

        // Revert old vote
        if (currentVote === 1)  upVotes   = delta(upVotes,   -1);
        if (currentVote === -1) downVotes = delta(downVotes, -1);

        // Apply new vote
        if (effectiveVote === 1)  upVotes   = delta(upVotes,   1);
        if (effectiveVote === -1) downVotes = delta(downVotes, 1);

        return {
          ...old,
          primary_code: {
            ...old.primary_code,
            up_votes:   upVotes,
            down_votes: downVotes,
          },
          viewer_state: {
            ...old.viewer_state,
            user_vote_on_primary_code:
              effectiveVote === 0 ? null : effectiveVote,
          },
        };
      });

      return { previousData };
    },

    onError: (_error, _variables, context) => {
      if (context?.previousData) {
        queryClient.setQueryData(
          bathroomDetailKeys.detail(bathroomId, user?.id),
          context.previousData
        );
      }
    },

    onSuccess: () => {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({
        queryKey: bathroomDetailKeys.codes(bathroomId, user?.id),
        refetchType: 'active',
      });
    },
  });
}

/**
 * useRateCleanliness
 *
 * Single rating per user per bathroom (upsert on server).
 * On success: invalidate detail (avg_cleanliness changes) + update viewer_state.
 */
export function useRateCleanliness(bathroomId: string) {
  const queryClient        = useQueryClient();
  const { user, isAuthenticated } = useAuth();

  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: async ({
      rating,
      notes,
    }: {
      rating: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5;
      notes?: string;
    }) => {
      if (!isAuthenticated || !user?.id) {
        await returnIntent.set({
          type:            'rate_cleanliness',
          route:           `/bathroom/${bathroomId}`,
          params:          { bathroom_id: bathroomId, rating },
          replay_strategy: 'immediate_after_auth',
        });
        throw new Error('AUTH_REQUIRED');
      }
      return bathroomService.rateCleanliness(bathroomId, rating, notes);
    },

    onSuccess: () => {
      // Invalidate detail — avg_cleanliness and viewer_state both change
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({
        queryKey: bathroomDetailKeys.detail(bathroomId, user?.id),
        refetchType: 'active',
      });
    },
  });
}
```
